title: Dashboard summary endpoint contract mismatch — getDashboardSummary() throws on every call, dashboard stays empty
detail: Flutter DashboardSummary.fromJson does non-nullable casts json['protection'] as Map, json['threats'] as Map, and json['device_health'] as Map (threat_stats.dart:106,107,117). But backend AlertsHandler.GetDashboard (wired to GET /api/v1/stats/dashboard in router.go:70) returns keys threat_score, protection_grade, active_threats, recent_alerts, threat_trend, protection_status, last_scan — NONE of protection, threats, or device_health. The cast throws a TypeError on every response; DashboardProvider._fetchDashboardSummary (dashboard_provider.dart:141-148) swallows it and returns null. Net effect: ProtectionStatusCard, ThreatStatsCard.threatOverview, RecentAlertsWidget, and DeviceHealthCard on the Dashboard, plus Security Center protection summary and recent alerts, are ALL permanently empty regardless of real data. Backend also hardcodes threat_score:85.0 and grade 'B' (alerts.go:158-159).
fix: Make backend GetDashboard match the DashboardSummary contract: emit nested protection object (is_protected, protection_score, protection_grade, sms/web/app/network/vpn_protection FeatureStatus objects, last_scan), threats object (threats_blocked_today/week, active_campaigns, high_severity_threats), recent_alerts array of {id,title,message,severity,type,timestamp,is_read}, and device_health object (overall_score,is_healthy,...), populated from repositories. Alternatively relax DashboardSummary.fromJson to ?? {} like ProtectionOverview, but the real fix is emitting real nested data.

title: Dashboard 'Scan' button is a hardcoded empty placeholder — scanning from dashboard never scans
detail: _navigateToScan constructs ScanningScreen(onScan: () async => []) with comment '// Placeholder - integrate with actual scan'. ProtectionStatusCard scan CTA (dashboard_screen.dart:152 onScanTap) routes here, so tapping Scan shows the full fake scanning animation and always returns 'No threats detected'. The real scan exists in main.dart:357 _performScan (native platform.invokeMethod scanNetwork/scanProcesses/scanFileSystem/scanDatabases/scanMemory) but the dashboard never calls it.
fix: Wire the dashboard scan to the real routine: lift _performScan into a shared service/provider and pass it as onScan, or navigate to the same _startScan flow used in main.dart:311. Remove the () async => [] placeholder.

title: scanning_screen progress and 'items scanned' are entirely faked with random numbers / time-based animation
detail: _startCountTimer increments _itemsScanned += Random().nextInt(15) + 5 every 50ms (line 195), and _overallProgress is advanced purely by _animateProgress on a fixed timer across 6 cosmetic phases (lines 125-138,200-212) with Future.delayed(800 + Random().nextInt(400)) per phase. The displayed % and 'N items scanned' have no relationship to actual widget.onScan() work, which runs only once after all fake animation completes (line 142). Phase labels (System Analysis, Network Security, etc.) are decorative, not tied to actual scan stages. This presents fabricated scan telemetry.
fix: Drive progress and item counts from the real scan: have onScan (native _performScan) emit progress/stage/items-scanned via a Stream/callback and bind the UI to real values. Map the 6 visual phases to actual native modules (scanNetwork, scanProcesses, scanFileSystem, scanDatabases, scanMemory, threat-intel), advancing only as each completes.

title: analytics_service.go returns hardcoded/simulated data for almost every analytics endpoint
detail: Nearly the entire service is fabricated and served as real via /api/v1/analytics/* (router.go:679-689): generateTrendData synthesizes Count: 100 + i*10 etc. (137-144, comment 'Simulated data - in production, query database'); generateSeverityDistribution/TypeDistribution/PlatformDistribution/SourceDistribution return fixed literals (153-193); generateTopIndicators/TopDomains/TopIPs return hardcoded 'malicious-domain.com','185.220.101.1' etc. (196-267); generateMitreData hardcoded (296-304); GetAlertMetrics returns fixed 1250 alerts/85 open/MTTR 45.5 (307-332); GetDetectionMetrics returns fixed 1.5M checks/45000 detections (335-354); GetSourceHealth returns 3 hardcoded sources (357-401); GetGeoDistribution returns 5 hardcoded countries (404-414). Only generateSummary partially uses real repo stats (80-89).
fix: Implement real aggregations against indicators/alerts/sources/campaigns repositories with time-range filtering (parseTimeRange already passes Start/End). Trend = GROUP BY time bucket on created_at; distributions = SQL GROUP BY type/severity/platform/source; top domains/IPs from hit-count tables; geo from indicator geo enrichment; alert/detection metrics from the alerts store. Remove all literal return slices.

title: Hardcoded placeholder detection rate / false-positive rate / change metrics in analytics summary
detail: generateSummary sets summary.DetectionRate = 98.5 and summary.FalsePositiveRate = 0.3 with comment '// Calculate detection rate (placeholder)', and ChangeFromPrevious is hardcoded IndicatorsChange:15.2, CampaignsChange:5.0, CriticalChange:-8.3, Direction:'up' (100-105). previousStart/previousEnd are computed (96-98) then discarded via _ =. These fake KPIs surface in threats analytics summary and mirror getDetectionMetrics hardcoded 3.0/0.33 (line 340-342).
fix: Compute detection rate and false-positive rate from real detection/feedback counts (detections vs total checks; confirmed false positives vs detections). Compute ChangeFromPrevious by querying the previous period (already calculated) and diffing indicator/campaign/critical counts.

title: Analytics screen reads field names the backend never sends — data silently discarded
detail: Overview tab reads summary['threats_blocked'], summary['urls_checked'], summary['sms_analyzed'] (153-155) but AnalyticsSummary (analytics.go:49-69) has no such fields, so they always fall back to provider values. It reads _threatAnalytics['type_distribution'] with item keys name/count (158,165-166) but backend sends by_type with CategoryCount key category (analytics.go:26,94), so that branch never matches and always uses the else fallback. Geo reads countries[].country and _geoData['total'] (278,280) but GeoCountryData sends country_code/country_name and has no top-level total (analytics.go:338-346), so geo bars render 'Unknown' and divide-by-1.
fix: Align names: update the Flutter screen to read by_type (key category) and geo country_name/country_code, and compute geo total client-side by summing counts. Add real threats_blocked/urls_checked/sms_analyzed to AnalyticsSummary if those KPIs are intended, otherwise remove the dead reads.

title: Protection feature flags hardcoded in stats.go protection-status computation
detail: computeProtectionStatus hardcodes smsEnabled=true, webEnabled=true, appEnabled=true, networkEnabled=true, vpnEnabled=false with comment '// Feature flags (temporary hardcoded)'. These feed GET /api/v1/stats/protection (router.go:69) -> ProtectionStatus.Features, driving the Analytics 'Protection' tab module toggles and the protection score (-10 because vpn forced false). Every device sees identical fabricated feature states; toggling protection in-app has no effect on this response.
fix: Source feature flags from the device's actual enabled-modules state (persist per-device protection config and look it up by device_id/auth context) or a real settings store, and compute the protection score from the actual enabled set rather than constants.

title: Analytics reports persistence is in-memory only and report generation is a sleep stub with a 404 download URL
detail: AnalyticsService stores reports in reports map[string]*AnalyticsReport with comment '// Report storage (in production, use database)' (22-23). CreateReport/GetReport/ListReports operate only on this map, so reports are lost on restart and not shared across instances. generateReport (446-466) just time.Sleep(2*time.Second), marks Completed, and sets DownloadURL /api/v1/analytics/reports/{id}/download — but no such download route is registered (router.go:686-688 only has GET /reports/{id}), so the advertised link 404s and no report file is ever produced.
fix: Persist reports to a DB table (and generated files to object storage). Implement generateReport to actually render the requested report (PDF/CSV/JSON) from real analytics queries and store it. Register the /reports/{id}/download route with a handler that streams the artifact, or stop returning a DownloadURL.

title: Dead UI controls in Security Center and Dashboard (no-op onTap/onPressed)
detail: Security Center notifications bell has onTap: () { HapticFeedback.lightImpact(); // Show notifications } with no navigation (197-200); alerts 'View All' has empty onTap: () { // Navigate to full history } (884-886). Dashboard settings icon onTap: () { // Navigate to settings } is empty (126-130), as are RecentAlertsWidget onViewAll (171-173) and ThreatStatsCard onTap (161-163). These visible, tappable controls do nothing.
fix: Wire each to its destination (notifications/alert-history list, settings screen, threat-details). For 'View All' alerts, navigate to a full alerts list backed by GET /api/v1/alerts (already implemented in the API client).

title: indicators type mismatch drops entire server response → silent local fallback
location: APP lib/providers/scam_detection_provider.dart:217 (indicators: List<String>.from(response['indicators'] ?? [])) vs LAB internal/domain/models/scam_analysis.go:110 (Indicators []ScamIndicator) and scam_detector.go:200-205
detail: The backend returns indicators as an array of objects {type, description, confidence, evidence}. The Flutter provider parses them with List<String>.from(...), which throws a TypeError as soon as the server returns any indicator. Because analyzeContent() wraps the parse in a try/catch whose catch runs _localAnalysis(), ANY successful backend analysis that contains indicators is discarded and the app instead shows its own on-device keyword heuristic. So the 'AI-powered' result the user sees is almost never the backend's result — it is the local fallback. This is a core-feature-returns-wrong-source defect.
fix: In the provider, map each indicator object to a display string: indicators: (response['indicators'] as List?)?.map((e) => e is Map ? (e['description'] ?? e['type']).toString() : e.toString()).toList() ?? []. Alternatively change the backend Analyze handler to also emit a flat string array (e.g. indicator_descriptions) that the client consumes. Either way the contract must agree on shape.

title: ScamDetector built with empty config — LLM, pattern DB, phone, vision, speech all disabled
detail: ScamDetectorConfig{} leaves EnableLLM=false, EnablePatternDB=false, EnablePhoneRep=false, EnableVision=false, EnableSpeech=false and all API keys empty. Consequences traced in scam_detector.go: patternDB is nil so analyzeText() skips ALL pattern matching (the entire ScamPatternDB engine is dead code at runtime); llmClient is nil so no Claude/OpenAI deep analysis ever runs; phoneReputation is nil so analyzePhone() returns 'phone reputation service not enabled'; analyzeImage()/analyzeVoice() return 'vision/speech not enabled'. The only live logic is intent_classifier rule matching + entity_extractor regex. The headline 'AI-powered' detection is effectively a keyword matcher.
fix: Populate ScamDetectorConfig from cfg/env: set EnablePatternDB=true, EnableLLM=true with ClaudeAPIKey/OpenAIAPIKey from config, EnablePhoneRep=true, EnableVision/EnableSpeech=true where keys exist, and ScamThreshold/SuspiciousThresh. Wire these from the app config struct the same way other services in main.go are configured, and log which capabilities are enabled at startup.

title: Confidence field never populated — app always shows 0% confidence
location: LAB internal/domain/services/ai/scam_detector.go (no result.Confidence assignment anywhere; finalizeResult at :582-606 sets only RiskScore-derived fields) consumed by APP lib/providers/scam_detection_provider.dart:215 (confidence: (response['confidence'] ?? 0.0).toDouble())
detail: The detector populates result.RiskScore throughout but never sets result.Confidence. The JSON field 'confidence' (scam_analysis.go:103) therefore serializes as 0. The Flutter result card renders '${(confidence*100).toInt()}% confidence' and riskLevel/riskColor are derived from confidence, so even a detected scam shows '0% confidence' and is colored as low/yellow risk. This is a user-visible correctness bug on every server-backed result.
fix: In finalizeResult set result.Confidence = result.RiskScore (or compute a distinct confidence). Alternatively have the client read 'risk_score' instead of 'confidence'. The two fields must be reconciled so the displayed percentage reflects the real score.

title: Patterns endpoint contract mismatch — app ignores server, uses hardcoded fake counts
location: APP lib/services/api/orbguard_api_client.dart:442-449 + provider :171-187 (reads response.data['patterns'] expecting id/name/description/type/keywords/detection_count) vs LAB internal/api/handlers/scam_detection.go:111-135 (returns 'scam_types' array with type/description/indicators and no id/name/keywords/detection_count)
detail: getScamPatterns() casts response.data['patterns'] which does not exist (backend key is 'scam_types'), so it returns []. loadPatterns() then hits its catch and loads _getDefaultPatterns() — five hardcoded patterns with invented detectionCount values (15420, 23150, etc.). The Patterns tab thus always shows static fabricated data, never the backend pattern catalog. The backend pattern payload is also static (no DB, no real detection counts).
fix: Align the shapes: either change the handler to return key 'patterns' with objects {id,name,description,type,keywords,detection_count} sourced from the real ScamPatternDB (and expose actual report/detection counts), or change the client to read 'scam_types' and map its fields. Remove the silent fallback to hardcoded counts so missing data surfaces instead of masquerading as real.

title: Phone reputation path mismatch + disabled analyzer — phone checks always return 'safe'
location: APP lib/services/api/orbguard_api_client.dart:462-469 (GET '${ApiEndpoints.scam}/phone/reputation' ?number=) vs LAB internal/api/handlers/scam_detection.go:36 (scam.Get('/phone/{number}')) and api_config.dart:820-821 (scamPhoneReputation builds /scam/phone/{number}, but the client method ignores it)
detail: The client requests /scam/phone/reputation?number=X, so chi binds {number}='reputation' (the actual phone number in the query string is ignored). Worse, even with a correct path the handler calls detector.Analyze with ContentTypePhone, which hits analyzePhone() — and because phoneReputation is nil (empty config), it returns an error and the handler falls through to the hardcoded 'clean' response (reputation_score:100, is_scam:false). So every phone lookup reports the number as safe regardless of input. The defined helper api_config.scamPhoneReputation(number) is also unused (dead).
fix: Change the client to call ApiEndpoints.scamPhoneReputation(Uri.encodeComponent(phoneNumber)) (path param), enable the phone reputation service (EnablePhoneRep=true) and back it by a real datasource, and have the handler return an explicit error/unknown state instead of a fabricated reputation_score:100 when lookup is unavailable.

title: recommendations field never sent by server — recommendations always empty on backend results
location: APP lib/providers/scam_detection_provider.dart:219 (recommendations: List<String>.from(response['recommendations'] ?? [])) vs LAB internal/domain/models/scam_analysis.go:125-127 (Recommendation string / SafetyTips []string; no 'recommendations' array)
detail: The backend produces a single 'recommendation' string (generateRecommendation joins lines) and optional 'safety_tips', but the client reads a 'recommendations' array that the server never emits, so the result card's Recommendations section is always empty for server-backed results (the only recommendations users ever see come from the local fallback). Note this is currently masked by finding #1 (server results are discarded anyway), but it must be fixed alongside it.
fix: Have the client read 'recommendation' (split on newlines) and/or 'safety_tips' into the recommendations list, or change the backend to emit a 'recommendations' string array. Reconcile the field name and cardinality.

title: Voice characteristics analysis is a hardcoded placeholder
location: LAB internal/domain/services/ai/speech_analyzer.go:514-525 (analyzeVoiceCharacteristics returns fixed VoiceCharacteristics{SpeakerCount:1, IsSynthetic:false, AudioQuality:'unknown'...})
detail: analyzeVoiceCharacteristics returns constant dummy values with a 'This is a placeholder' comment. It is only invoked when config.EnableVoiceprint is true, which is never set, and the Voice content type in the UI renders 'coming soon' (scam_detection_screen.dart:248) and never submits audio. So this is dead/placeholder rather than user-facing today, but it is fake data presented as analysis if voiceprint is ever enabled.
fix: Implement real voice characteristic analysis (speaker count, synthetic-voice/voice-clone detection, emotion/urgency) via an audio-analysis library or external API, or remove VoiceCharacteristics from the response until implemented so it cannot return fabricated values.

title: Voice content type is a dead UI path ('coming soon')
location: APP lib/screens/scam/scam_detection_screen.dart:239-254 (_buildInputField default branch shows 'This content type analysis is coming soon') with ScamContentType.image and ScamContentType.voice (provider :11-13)
detail: The content-type selector exposes Image and Voice chips, but selecting them shows a 'coming soon' panel and the Analyze button no-ops (the _analyze switch has no image/voice cases, default: break). The backend has real (if currently disabled) image and speech analyzers, so the gap is purely client-side: no image-picker/audio-recorder is wired to base64-encode and POST ImageData/AudioData. This is a user-visible unfinished feature.
fix: Either hide the Image/Voice chips until implemented, or wire an image picker and audio recorder that base64-encode the media and send it (with content_type=image/voice and mime_type) to /scam/analyze, and enable EnableVision/EnableSpeech on the backend so these chains become functional end-to-end.

title: SMS analysis request field mismatch: client sends 'content', backend reads 'body'
detail: The Flutter client serializes the message text under key 'content' (toJson emits {'content': content, 'sender':..., 'timestamp':..., 'device_id':...}). The backend AnalyzeRequest struct only has json tags 'sender','body','timestamp','device_id' and validates `if req.Body == ""`. Since the client never sends 'body', every analyze request arrives with an empty Body and is rejected with HTTP 400 'Message body is required' (or analyzed as empty text). The single-message and batch analyze paths are both affected (batch wraps the same toJson).
fix: Make the contract consistent. Easiest: change SmsAnalysisRequest.toJson to emit 'body' instead of 'content' (and keep the Dart field name if desired). Alternatively add `Body string \`json:"content"\`` mapping or accept both keys in AnalyzeRequest. Verify with an integration test posting the exact client payload.
domain: SMS Protection (OrbGuard anti-spyware client + OrbGuard Lab threat-intel backend)

title: SMS analysis response shape mismatch — real results decode to empty/SAFE
detail: Even if a request succeeds, the backend response cannot be parsed correctly. Client expects: risk_score (num), extracted_urls[], sender_analysis{is_known_spammer,is_short_code,...}, detected_intents[] (string list), matched_patterns[] (string list), recommendation (single string), should_block (bool), threats[] with {type,severity,...}. Backend emits: confidence (not risk_score), urls (not extracted_urls), intent_analysis{} (an object, not a detected_intents string list), pattern_matches[] (objects, not strings), recommendations[] (array, not a single 'recommendation'), NO should_block, NO threats[] array (it has is_threat/threat_type/threat_level instead). Because fromJson uses null-safe defaults, the parsed result is always riskScore=0.0, empty threats, empty intents/patterns, null recommendation, shouldBlock=false. Backend threat_level values are safe/low/medium/high/critical, but the client enum only knows safe/suspicious/dangerous/critical — 'low','medium','high' fall back to SAFE. Result: a backend-flagged 'high' phishing SMS renders as SAFE with 0% risk in the UI.
fix: Align the DTOs. Either (a) add a response transformer/DTO in the LAB handler that maps the internal SMSAnalysisResult to the exact shape the Flutter client expects (risk_score, extracted_urls, detected_intents as strings derived from intent_analysis.suspicious_flags, matched_patterns as pattern_name strings, single recommendation joined or first-of recommendations, should_block = is_threat && level>=high, and remap level high/medium/low to suspicious/dangerous/critical), or (b) rewrite SmsAnalysisResult.fromJson + enums to consume the native backend shape (confidence, urls, intent_analysis, pattern_matches, recommendations, is_threat/threat_type, level low/medium/high). Add a golden-JSON test using a real handler response.
domain: SMS Protection (OrbGuard anti-spyware client + OrbGuard Lab threat-intel backend)

title: Inbox never loads device messages — loadMessages() is a no-op TODO
detail: loadMessages() sets loading flags and calls _updateStats() but contains only `// TODO: Load messages from native platform channel`. It never reads the SMS inbox. The inbox tab and pull-to-refresh therefore always show the empty state. The only messages that can ever appear are ones pushed via addMessage(), and nothing in the app calls addMessage for the provider used by the screen.
fix: Wire loadMessages() to SmsPlatformService.readSmsInbox() (the native readSmsInbox in android SMSAnalyzer.kt already returns real inbox rows), map results to SmsMessage, dedupe, and addMessage each. On iOS where inbox reading is unavailable, surface a clear 'manual check only' state instead of a silent empty list.
domain: SMS Protection (OrbGuard anti-spyware client + OrbGuard Lab threat-intel backend)

title: SmsPlatformService is orphaned — native SMS channel never connected to provider
location: APP lib/services/platform/sms_platform_service.dart (entire file) — no references outside itself; APP lib/screens/sms_protection/sms_protection_screen.dart:26 and lib/main.dart:115 create independent SmsProvider() instances
detail: SmsPlatformService implements the working bridge to the native 'com.orb.guard/sms' channel (which IS registered in android MainActivity.kt:699-768, with real readSmsInbox/checkSmsPermission/requestSmsPermission). But the class is never instantiated, init() never called, and setSmsProvider() never invoked anywhere in lib/. Incoming-SMS push (onSmsReceived), permission requests, and inbox reads are thus all unreachable from the UI. Additionally the screen instantiates its own `final SmsProvider _provider = SmsProvider()` separate from the main.dart ChangeNotifierProvider instance, so even shared-state wiring is inconsistent.
fix: Instantiate SmsPlatformService at startup (e.g. in main.dart), call init(smsProvider: <the same provider the screen uses>) and setSmsProvider, and have the screen consume the Provider-injected SmsProvider instead of constructing a private one. Then loadMessages()/permission flow/real-time push all become functional.
domain: SMS Protection (OrbGuard anti-spyware client + OrbGuard Lab threat-intel backend)

title: report-false-positive route aliased to GetStats — reports are silently discarded
location: LAB internal/api/router.go:139 (sms.Post("/report-false-positive", r.handlers.SMS.GetStats)) called from APP lib/services/api/orbguard_api_client.dart:1077-1089
detail: The client posts {message_id, content} to /sms/report-false-positive expecting the report to improve detection. The router maps this POST to SMS.GetStats (commented '// Alias for now, returns stats'). The handler ignores the body and returns hardcoded zero stats. No false-positive is recorded, no learning/feedback occurs. The UI (sms_detail_screen.dart:87-118) shows 'Report submitted. Thank you!' regardless, misleading the user.
fix: Implement a real SMS.ReportFalsePositive handler that persists {message_id, content, sender, reported_at} to a feedback table (or pushes to the pattern/allowlist pipeline) and register it on the route. At minimum store the report and return 200; ideally feed it into pattern tuning / a per-user allowlist.
domain: SMS Protection (OrbGuard anti-spyware client + OrbGuard Lab threat-intel backend)

title: GetStats returns hardcoded zeros — SMS stats endpoint is a placeholder
location: LAB internal/api/handlers/sms.go:255-291 (GetStats); contract also exposed via APP api_config.dart:75 smsStats
detail: GetStats has comment '// In a real implementation, these would come from database' and returns TotalAnalyzed:0, ThreatsDetected:0, and all ThreatsByType/ThreatsByLevel maps set to 0. No DB query is performed. Any client/dashboard consuming /sms/stats gets fabricated empty data. (This endpoint is also double-used as the false-positive alias, compounding the issue.)
fix: Aggregate real counters from persisted analysis results (add persistence of analysis outcomes in Analyze/AnalyzeBatch, then COUNT/GROUP BY in GetStats), or remove the endpoint until backed by data. Do not return zeros styled as real metrics.
domain: SMS Protection (OrbGuard anti-spyware client + OrbGuard Lab threat-intel backend)

title: Blocked-sender and stats persistence are TODO stubs — state lost every launch
detail: loadBlockedSenders() resets _blockedSenders to [] with '// TODO: Load from persistent storage'. _saveBlockedSenders() body is just '// TODO: Save to persistent storage' (blockSender/unblockSender call it but nothing is written). loadStats() only calls _updateStats(). Consequently every block the user makes (and the blocked-senders sheet, the auto-block-on-dangerous UX, and stats) is in-memory only and wiped on app restart. The native autoBlock setting (updateSettings) is also never invoked from the provider.
fix: Persist blocked senders and stats via SharedPreferences/secure storage (or sync to backend). Implement _saveBlockedSenders to write the list and loadBlockedSenders to read it back on init(); have loadStats restore last counters. Optionally push the blocklist to the native layer so OS-level filtering can act on it.
domain: SMS Protection (OrbGuard anti-spyware client + OrbGuard Lab threat-intel backend)

title: SMSScannerService channel not registered natively — service is dead code
location: APP lib/services/security/sms_scanner_service.dart:127-128 (channels 'com.orbvpn.orbguard/sms_scanner' / 'com.orbvpn.orbguard/sms_events'); no references in android/ ios/ macos/ and no callers in lib/ outside the file
detail: SMSScannerService uses MethodChannel 'com.orbvpn.orbguard/sms_scanner' and EventChannel 'com.orbvpn.orbguard/sms_events'. Neither channel is registered in any native code (grep over android/ios/macos finds nothing), and the class is never imported/used by any screen or provider. Its initialize/startMonitoring/scanAllMessages calls would throw MissingPluginException. It duplicates (with a different local heuristic) the analysis the backend already performs, but is entirely unreachable.
fix: Either delete SMSScannerService (and consolidate on SmsProvider + SmsPlatformService + backend analyzer) or register the matching native channels and wire it into the UI. Leaving an unwired second SMS pipeline with mismatched channel names is misleading and risks runtime crashes if ever called.
domain: SMS Protection (OrbGuard anti-spyware client + OrbGuard Lab threat-intel backend)

title: On-device SmsClassifier ML never invoked by SMS domain; model is heuristic-only stub
location: APP lib/services/ml/sms_classifier.dart:87-98 (loadModel TODO) and lib/services/ml/ml_service.dart:107-111 (classifySms) — no callers of classifySms in the SMS screens/provider
detail: SmsClassifier.loadModel() never loads the declared TFLite asset (assets/models/sms_phishing.tflite); it just sets _modelVersion='1.0.0-heuristic' (comment '// TODO: Load actual TFLite model when available'). classify() always uses _classifyHeuristic and returns usedFallback:true. More importantly, ml_service.classifySms()/classifySmsBatch() are never called from the SMS protection screens or SmsProvider (grep finds no non-ml callers), so the advertised on-device ML phishing detection is not part of the live SMS flow at all — offline analysis is impossible and the model version reported in diagnostics is misleading.
fix: If on-device classification is a product requirement: ship the real TFLite model + vocab and implement inference in loadModel/classify, then call ml_service.classifySms() in analyzeContent/analyzeMessage as an offline fallback when the API is unreachable. If not required, remove the model-version messaging that implies a loaded ML model and drop classifySms from the public surface.
domain: SMS Protection (OrbGuard anti-spyware client + OrbGuard Lab threat-intel backend)

title: Protection-status panel shows hardcoded 'Active/Enabled/Connected'
detail: The Stats tab renders SMS Monitoring='Active', Real-time Analysis='Enabled', Cloud Intelligence='Connected' as static green labels with no backing state. Given that real-time monitoring (SmsPlatformService/SMSScannerService) is not wired and the API contract is broken, these statuses are false reassurance to the user.
fix: Drive these rows from real state: SMS permission granted + platform service initialized (monitoring), provider initialized (analysis), and a lightweight API health/ping (cloud intelligence). Show red/'Inactive' when the underlying capability is unavailable.
domain: SMS Protection (OrbGuard anti-spyware client + OrbGuard Lab threat-intel backend)

title: URL check response contract mismatch drops severity, risk score, categories, threats, recommendation
detail: Dart UrlReputationResult.fromJson reads json['severity'], json['risk_score'], json['categories'] (array), json['threats'] (array), json['recommendation']. The Go URLCheckResponse emits NONE of these: it has `category` (singular string, json:"category"), `threat_level` (json:"threat_level"), `confidence`, `description`, `warnings`, `block_reason`. Result: every real check decodes to severity=info, riskScore=0.0, categories=[], threats=[], recommendation=null regardless of the backend verdict. The is_safe/should_block booleans round-trip, but the whole risk/severity/category presentation in UrlResultCard is wrong/empty for genuine threats.
fix: Align the field names. Either (a) add JSON aliases on the Go response: map Category->categories (wrap singular into a one-element array or add a `categories` array field), ThreatLevel->severity, Confidence->risk_score (or add risk_score), Warnings/Description->threats+recommendation; or (b) change the Dart model to read category (singular), threat_level, confidence, warnings, block_reason and synthesize categories/threats from them. Add a round-trip integration test asserting a known-phishing URL yields severity=high and a non-empty category in the parsed Dart object.

title: URL stats counters always zero because categories never populate
detail: _updateStats() computes phishingBlocked/malwareBlocked/scamsBlocked by iterating entry.result!.categories and matching UrlCategory.phishing/malware/scam. Because of the contract mismatch above, categories is always empty, so these counters are permanently 0. threatsBlocked counts !isSafe entries so it can be non-zero, but the per-category breakdown shown in UrlStatsCard on the Stats tab is dead.
fix: After fixing the response contract so categories is populated (or by deriving category from the singular `category`/`threat_level` field), recompute stats. If keeping singular category, change _updateStats to read result.primaryCategory / a single category field instead of the categories list.

title: Google Safe Browsing client wired as nil — real-time Google verdicts never used
detail: A complete GoogleSafeBrowsingClient exists in internal/domain/services/safe_browsing.go implementing SafeBrowsingClient, but main passes nil. In url_reputation.go runChecks() the `if s.safeBrowsing != nil` guard (line 196) means checkSafeBrowsing never runs in production. The marketed Google Safe Browsing integration is therefore inert; detection relies solely on local heuristics/regex.
fix: Construct services.NewGoogleSafeBrowsingClient(SafeBrowsingConfig{APIKey: cfg.SafeBrowsing.APIKey, ...}, log) and pass it instead of nil (gate on whether the API key is configured). Add config plumbing for the key. Verify with a known EICAR/test malware URL that checkSafeBrowsing flips IsThreat.

title: GoogleSafeBrowsingClient builds request with nil body then sets req.Body without Content-Length
detail: http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, "POST", url, nil) is called with a nil body, then req.Body = newBodyReader(jsonBody) is assigned manually. This leaves req.ContentLength=0 and GetBody unset; depending on the reader it can send a 0-length or chunked body that Google's API may reject. Standard practice is to pass the body reader to NewRequestWithContext so Content-Length/GetBody are set correctly.
fix: Pass bytes.NewReader(jsonBody) as the body argument to http.NewRequestWithContext instead of nil, and remove the manual req.Body assignment. This also fixes retries/redirects which need GetBody.

title: Whitelist/blacklist not persisted on backend; RemoveFromList is a no-op
location: LAB internal/domain/services/url_reputation.go:710-715 (RemoveFromList no-op), 533-567 (Add* write only to in-memory maps)
detail: AddToWhitelist/AddToBlacklist only mutate the in-memory knownBadDomains/whitelistedDomains maps; nothing is written to the repository/DB. RemoveFromList explicitly logs 'no-op in memory' and returns nil without removing anything (the DELETE /url/list/{id} endpoint always 204s but does nothing). GetList synthesizes fresh UUIDs each call (line 687/700) so the IDs returned can never be used to delete. All custom list state is lost on server restart and is not per-user.
fix: Add a url_list repository (table url_list_entries) and persist Add/Remove/Get through it keyed by stable UUID and ideally by device/user. Make RemoveFromList delete by the real stored ID. Make GetList return stored entries with their persistent IDs, not freshly generated ones.

title: Provider whitelist/blacklist & history are in-memory only and never call backend; persistence methods are TODO stubs
detail: loadHistory(), _saveHistory(), loadLists(), _saveLists() are empty bodies with `// TODO: Load/Save from persistent storage`. addToWhitelist/addToBlacklist mutate local lists and call _saveLists() (no-op) — they never call the existing backend endpoints (/url/whitelist, /url/blacklist) which the OrbGuardApiClient doesn't even expose methods for. So check history and custom lists vanish on every app restart, and lists are not synced to the backend/VPN DNS rules. The UI ListManagement sheet and whitelist/blacklist buttons therefore have no durable effect.
fix: Implement loadHistory/_saveHistory and loadLists/_saveLists using shared_preferences or a local DB (sqflite/hive). Add OrbGuardApiClient methods for POST /url/whitelist, POST /url/blacklist, GET/DELETE list endpoints and call them from the provider so lists persist and feed GetDNSBlockRules for the VPN. Wire the whitelist add to actually reach the backend.

title: GET /url/reputation/{domain} returns 404 for virtually all real domains (WHOIS/SSL/DNS enrichment unimplemented)
location: LAB internal/domain/services/url_reputation.go:354-366 (checkDomainReputation stub) and 575-632 (GetDomainReputation), APP lib/models/api/url_reputation.dart:166-186 (DomainReputation expects whois/ssl/dns/age/nameservers)
detail: GetDomainReputation only returns data if the domain is in the threat-intel DB, the static whitelist, or the in-memory blacklist; otherwise it returns (nil,nil) and the handler responds 404 'no reputation data for domain'. checkDomainReputation is a stub (comment: 'This would typically integrate with WHOIS data... For now, we flag new/unknown domains' but does nothing except the in-memory blacklist check). Meanwhile the Dart DomainReputation model expects rich fields: created_date, expires_date, age_in_days, registrar, nameservers, ip_addresses, asn, reputation_score, whois, ssl, dns. None are ever produced. The 'Show domain details' tap (screen line 66 _showDomainDetails) thus fails or shows empty data for normal sites.
fix: Implement real domain enrichment (WHOIS lookup, DNS resolution, TLS cert inspection) populating URLReputation's whois/ssl/dns/age fields, OR return a minimal-but-real reputation (computed from the heuristic check) for unknown domains instead of 404 so the details sheet always has content. At minimum, have GetDomainReputation fall back to CheckURL-derived data rather than nil.

title: SafeBrowsingService and PhishTankService (Flutter) are dead code never instantiated
location: APP lib/services/api/safe_browsing_service.dart (entire file), lib/services/api/phishtank_service.dart (entire file; submitPhish placeholder at line 384-389)
detail: Neither SafeBrowsingService nor PhishTankService is imported or instantiated anywhere in the app (grep finds zero construction sites). They are fully-written but unused. PhishTankService.submitPhish is an explicit placeholder that prints and returns false. The URL chain uses only the backend /url/check. This is significant dead surface presented as functionality (the app appears to have client-side Google Safe Browsing + PhishTank, but does not run them).
fix: Either wire these into UrlProvider.checkUrl as supplementary on-device checks (instantiate with real API keys, combine results) or delete them to avoid implying capabilities that don't run. If kept, implement submitPhish or remove it.

title: On-device URL ML classifier is a heuristic placeholder with no model and is unused in URL Protection
location: APP lib/services/ml/url_classifier.dart:87-97 (loadModel TODO, _modelVersion '1.0.0-heuristic', usedFallback always true)
detail: UrlClassifier.loadModel() has 'TODO: Load actual TFLite model when available' and just sets _isModelLoaded=true while only ever running _classifyHeuristic (usedFallback:true on every result). The assets/models/url_threat.tflite model is never loaded. It is instantiated by MLService but is NOT used anywhere in the URL Protection screen/provider chain, so the 'on-device ML URL detection' is both a placeholder and disconnected from the URL feature.
fix: Ship and load the real TFLite model in loadModel(), or relabel/remove the ML claim. If on-device classification is desired in URL Protection, call MLService/UrlClassifier from UrlProvider.checkUrl and merge its verdict with the backend result.

title: ReportURL only logs reports; no persistence or review pipeline
detail: ReportURL (POST /url/report) for false_positive/missed_threat just writes a log line and returns 'Thank you for your report.' Nothing is stored, queued, or routed to analysts; the promised 'It will be reviewed' is not backed by any mechanism. The app's whitelist/blacklist buttons don't call this either.
fix: Persist reports to a url_reports table (or push to the event bus/queue) so they can actually be triaged, and dedupe by url+device. Optionally feed confirmed reports into the indicators/blocklist pipeline.

title: Safe Browsing 'API error' and network errors are swallowed as safe in unused Flutter service
detail: In _lookupUrls, both non-200 API responses and network exceptions return URLCheckResult(isSafe:true) (fail-open) while only setting an `error` string that callers ignore. For a security feature this fails open silently. (Currently moot because the service is dead code, but if wired per the earlier finding it would mark threats as safe on any transient error.)
fix: On API/network error, surface an explicit 'unknown/unable-to-verify' state rather than isSafe:true, and propagate the error so the UI can warn the user instead of implying the URL is safe.

title: Identity Protection is a fully local simulation — credit scores never populated
detail: IdentityProtectionProvider sources ALL data from IdentityTheftProtectionService, which is an in-memory mock with no backend wiring. getCreditScores() returns the _creditScores map, but updateCreditScore() — the only method that ever fills that map — has ZERO callers in the entire codebase (grep confirms). The identity screen (identity_protection_screen.dart:838 `provider.creditScores[bureau]`) therefore always renders null/empty bureau score cards. The whole 'Credit Bureau Scores' section is dead.
fix: Wire credit monitoring to a real bureau/aggregator API (e.g. an Array/CRS-style provider) via a backend endpoint (add /api/v1/identity/credit-scores in LAB and a method in orbguard_api_client.dart), populate _creditScores from that response on initialize()/scan, and remove the unused local updateCreditScore mock or have a scheduled job feed it. If no bureau integration is licensed, hide the credit-score UI instead of showing empty cards.
domain: Dark Web / Digital Footprint / Identity / Privacy

title: Credit freeze/unfreeze and recovery cases mutate only in-memory state — no real action
detail: freezeCredit() just builds a CreditFreezeStatus object with a PIN derived from DateTime.now()%10^10 (not cryptographically secure despite the name) and stores it in a local map; it never contacts Equifax/Experian/TransUnion. unfreezeCredit() likewise. openRecoveryCase() creates a local object with canned FTC steps and no agent/case-management backend. The UI (identity_protection_screen.dart:416-418) presents these as functional Freeze/Unfreeze buttons, misleading the user into believing their credit is actually frozen.
fix: Either integrate real bureau freeze APIs / deep-link to the official bureau freeze pages and track confirmation, or relabel these as 'guided manual steps' that open the bureau URLs. Replace _generateSecurePin with SecureRandom. Back recovery cases with a real persisted endpoint.
domain: Dark Web / Digital Footprint / Identity / Privacy

title: Identity asset scanning returns empty arrays (dark web / data brokers / public records)
detail: scanAllAssets()/_scanAsset() call three private scanners that each contain only a comment ('In production, this would call a dark web monitoring API') and `return []`. So 'Scan all assets' in the identity screen always finds nothing and never raises an alert, while presenting a working scan spinner. The alertStream is therefore never fed from scanning.
fix: Route identity asset scans through the existing real backend: reuse DarkWebMonitor (HIBP) for email/phone exposure and the digital_footprint Scanner for broker/public-records checks, mapping results into IdentityAlert. Add the corresponding api_client calls and populate alerts from server responses.
domain: Dark Web / Digital Footprint / Identity / Privacy

title: Digital Footprint API path mismatch — Flutter calls /api/footprint/* but backend serves /api/v1/footprint/*
location: APP lib/services/api/orbguard_api_client.dart:1120-1163 (getDataBrokers, scanDigitalFootprint, requestDataRemoval, getRemovalStatus) vs LAB internal/api/router.go:614-624
detail: The footprint client methods hardcode paths '/api/footprint/brokers', '/api/footprint/scan', '/api/footprint/removal', '/api/footprint/removal/{id}' — all missing the '/v1' segment and not using ApiEndpoints constants. The router only registers /api/v1/footprint/*. Every footprint network call 404s, so DigitalFootprintProvider always hits its catch blocks and serves fake data.
fix: Change these 4 paths to '$_v1/footprint/...' (or add ApiEndpoints.footprint* constants matching api_config.dart's pattern). Verify against router.go: brokers=/api/v1/footprint/brokers, scan=/api/v1/footprint/scan, removal=/api/v1/footprint/removal, status=/api/v1/footprint/removal/{id}.
domain: Dark Web / Digital Footprint / Identity / Privacy

title: Footprint scan request omits include_* flags and sends wrong address shape → backend returns empty footprint
detail: Flutter sends {email, first_name, last_name, phone, address(String)}. The Go FootprintScanRequest requires IncludeDarkWeb/IncludeDataBrokers/IncludeSocialMedia booleans (default false) and Addresses []AddressInfo. Scanner.ScanFootprint only runs each sub-scan when its Include* flag AND req.IncludeX are true, so with the flags omitted every sub-scan is skipped and the result has 0 brokers/exposures. The single 'address' string is dropped entirely (wrong key + wrong type).
fix: In scanDigitalFootprint payload add include_dark_web:true, include_data_brokers:true, include_social_media:true, include_breaches:true, and convert address into an addresses:[{street,city,state,zip_code,country}] array. Keep full_name in sync with first/last.
domain: Dark Web / Digital Footprint / Identity / Privacy

title: Footprint provider fabricates fake scan results on any failure (12 brokers, score 45) with simulated progress
detail: scan() runs a cosmetic 10x300ms fake progress loop, then on ANY exception (which is guaranteed today due to the path/flag bugs above) returns a hardcoded FootprintScanResult with brokersFound:12, exposuresFound:8, privacyScore:45 built from _brokers.take(12). loadBrokers() similarly falls back to a hardcoded _getDefaultBrokers() list. The user sees convincing but entirely fabricated exposure numbers.
fix: Remove the simulated progress loop and the fabricated catch-block result; surface a real error state instead. Drive progress from a real backend scan-status/stream. Only show broker data returned by the server (GetAllBrokers endpoint already exists).
domain: Dark Web / Digital Footprint / Identity / Privacy

title: getBreachAlerts contract mismatch — Flutter reads 'alerts' key the backend never returns
detail: Flutter getBreachAlerts() does `response.data['alerts'] as List<dynamic>` (non-null cast). The backend GetAlerts responds with {"unread":[...],"read":[...],"unread_count":..,"total_count":..} and has no 'alerts' field. The cast of null throws, so DarkWebProvider.refreshAlerts() always lands in its catch and the breach-alerts list stays empty for every user.
fix: Either change the client to merge response.data['unread'] + response.data['read'], or change the handler to also return an 'alerts' array. Make the client null-safe (as List? ?? []).
domain: Dark Web / Digital Footprint / Identity / Privacy

title: Dark-web monitored assets & alerts stored in-memory, not persisted, keyed to unauthenticated user_id=default
detail: DarkWebMonitor stores assets and alerts in Go maps with an explicit comment 'would be database in production'. All data is lost on restart. Worse, the handlers default user_id to the literal string 'default' when the query param is absent (which the Flutter client never sends), so every device shares one global bucket of monitored assets/alerts — a privacy/data-leak issue and means cross-user monitoring is impossible.
fix: Persist monitored assets and alerts in Postgres keyed by the authenticated principal from the API key/JWT (not a query param). Replace the in-memory maps with a repository; derive user/device identity from auth middleware context.
domain: Dark Web / Digital Footprint / Identity / Privacy

title: Hardcoded production breach-API secrets committed in client source
detail: Real HIBP, LeakCheck, and Intelligence X API keys are hardcoded as string constants shipped in the Flutter client. Anyone decompiling the app can extract and abuse/exhaust these keys, and they appear in source control. These should never live client-side; the backend already has its own HIBPClient for server-side calls.
fix: Remove the keys from the client. Route all breach lookups through the backend (the /api/v1/darkweb/* endpoints already proxy HIBP server-side). Store provider keys only in backend config/secret store. Rotate the exposed keys immediately.
domain: Dark Web / Digital Footprint / Identity / Privacy

title: RequestRemoval handler never submits the opt-out — request stuck at 'pending' forever
location: LAB internal/api/handlers/footprint.go:126-155 (RequestRemoval) and services/digital_footprint/scanner.go:400-402 -> removal_service.go:50-87 (CreateRequest) vs removal_service.go:114-152 (ProcessRequest never called)
detail: RequestRemoval only calls Scanner.RequestRemoval -> RemovalService.CreateRequest, which creates a RemovalStatusPending record and returns. ProcessRequest (which actually submits the automated form / generates CCPA/GDPR letters) is never invoked from any handler path, so no opt-out is ever actually performed. GetRemovalStatus will always report 'pending'. The Flutter UI presents removal as an actioned request.
fix: After CreateRequest, enqueue/run ProcessRequest (sync or via a worker) so the opt-out form is submitted or the CCPA/GDPR letter is generated and the status advances. Persist requests (see in-memory note below) and expose the generated letter content to the client.
domain: Dark Web / Digital Footprint / Identity / Privacy

title: Footprint removal requests stored in in-memory sync.Map (not persisted)
location: LAB internal/domain/services/digital_footprint/removal_service.go:27-28 (requests sync.Map, 'until DB repo is available') and 78,156-160
detail: CreateRequest stores into an in-memory sync.Map and GetStatus reads from it. All removal-request history is lost on restart, and status polling fails after a redeploy. Explicit comment acknowledges DB repo is missing.
fix: Add a Postgres-backed removal-request repository and persist/read requests there, keyed by authenticated user.
domain: Dark Web / Digital Footprint / Identity / Privacy

title: Privacy clipboard-check path mismatch (and event recording missing required device_id)
location: APP lib/services/api/orbguard_api_client.dart:418-425 (checkClipboard -> /privacy/check/clipboard) & 408-415 (recordPrivacyEvent) vs LAB router.go:410,414 and handlers/privacy.go:29-51
detail: Client posts to '${ApiEndpoints.privacy}/check/clipboard' = /api/v1/privacy/check/clipboard, but the backend route is /api/v1/privacy/clipboard/check (segments swapped) → 404, so PrivacyProvider.checkClipboard always falls back to local regex-only crypto-address detection. Separately, recordPrivacyEvent() posts {type,app_name,package_name,is_background,timestamp} with no device_id, but RecordEvent returns 400 'device_id is required', so privacy analytics events are silently dropped (error swallowed via .catchError((_){})).
fix: Fix the client path to /api/v1/privacy/clipboard/check. Add device_id to the recordPrivacyEvent payload (from the registered device id) and align field names with models.PrivacyEvent. Stop swallowing the error silently or at least log it.
domain: Dark Web / Digital Footprint / Identity / Privacy

title: Privacy tracker list and audit fall back to hardcoded/heuristic data, hiding backend failures
detail: loadTrackers() on any API error loads an 8-item hardcoded _getDefaultTrackers() list. runAudit() on error fabricates a PrivacyAuditResult (score 75, appsWithTrackers:5, canned recommendations). Because there is no surfaced error, a broken /privacy/audit or /apps/trackers call looks like a successful audit with invented numbers.
fix: Surface real errors to the UI instead of silently substituting fabricated audit scores. Hardcoded tracker seed is acceptable only as an offline cache clearly labeled as such; the audit fabrication should be removed.
domain: Dark Web / Digital Footprint / Identity / Privacy

title: darkweb_provider asset persistence is empty TODO — monitored assets lost on relaunch and never synced to backend
detail: loadAssets() and _saveAssets() are empty `// TODO` stubs, so monitored assets exist only in RAM for the session. addAsset() also never calls the backend AddMonitoredAsset endpoint (POST /api/v1/darkweb/monitor) — it only does an immediate email breach check — so server-side continuous monitoring/alerting is never set up for assets the user adds. removeAsset likewise never calls DELETE /darkweb/monitor/{id}.
fix: Implement local persistence (shared_preferences/secure storage) for assets AND call POST /api/v1/darkweb/monitor on add / DELETE on remove so the backend actually monitors them and generates BreachAlerts. Load assets from GET /api/v1/darkweb/monitor on init.
domain: Dark Web / Digital Footprint / Identity / Privacy

title: GetBreaches / GetBreachByName handlers return placeholder messages instead of data
detail: Both handlers ignore the available HIBPClient.GetAllBreaches/GetBreachByName and instead respond with literal placeholder strings ('To list all breaches, configure HIBP API key' / 'Breach details would be fetched from HIBP'). The /api/v1/darkweb/breaches and /breaches/{name} endpoints thus return no real breach catalog.
fix: Call h.monitor's HIBP client (expose GetAllBreaches/GetBreachByName through DarkWebMonitor) and return the real breach list/detail, with a clear 503 only when the API key is genuinely unset.
domain: Dark Web / Digital Footprint / Identity / Privacy

title: darkweb_provider markAlertAsRead / markAllAlertsAsRead are no-ops (no API call, immutable model not updated)
detail: markAlertAsRead() finds the index but only calls _updateStats()/notifyListeners() with a comment that BreachAlert is immutable and 'would need API call'; it never calls the backend ack endpoint (POST /api/v1/darkweb/alerts/{id}/ack) and never flips isRead. markAllAlertsAsRead() does nothing meaningful. So 'mark as read' UI actions have no effect; unread counts never change.
fix: Call POST /api/v1/darkweb/alerts/{id}/ack (add the method to orbguard_api_client.dart) and rebuild the alert with isRead=true (use copyWith or refetch) so the read state persists and unread counts update.
domain: Dark Web / Digital Footprint / Identity / Privacy

title: Threat level contract mismatch makes dangerous QR codes display as SAFE
detail: Backend QRScanResult.ThreatLevel emits one of: safe/low/medium/high/critical (models.QRThreatLevel). QrScanResult.fromJson parses it via SmsThreatLevel.fromString (line 426), but that enum only defines safe/suspicious/dangerous/critical with an orElse fallback to SmsThreatLevel.safe (line 20). Therefore any backend verdict of 'low', 'medium', or 'high' parses to SAFE. The backend correctly flags typosquatting, IP-host URLs, premium-rate phones, open WiFi, invalid crypto addresses, and threat-intel hits as 'high' or 'medium' — all of these are silently shown to the user as safe/green. The whole point of the feature (warning about malicious QR codes) is defeated for the entire medium/high band. Also QrResultCard only shows the 'Report False Positive' and threat panel when threatLevel.index>1 (qr_scanner_screen.dart:212, qr_widgets.dart:142), so for high/medium results no threat UI appears at all.
fix: Introduce a dedicated QrThreatLevel enum mirroring the backend (safe, low, medium, high, critical) with proper colors/displayNames, and parse threat_level into it in QrScanResult.fromJson. Map low->index1, medium->2, high->3, critical->4 so the existing index>1 gating still surfaces medium+ threats. Do NOT reuse SmsThreatLevel, whose value set differs. Alternatively, change the backend to emit suspicious/dangerous — but the client enum is the correct place to fix since the backend taxonomy is the threat-intel canonical one.

title: Scanned content never binds (raw_content vs content) — result card shows empty content
detail: Backend serializes the scanned payload as `raw_content` (json:"raw_content"). QrScanResult.fromJson reads json['content'] (line 423), which is absent, so QrScanResult.content is always ''. QrResultCard renders result.content (qr_widgets.dart:59) and the Copy button copies result.content (qr_scanner_screen.dart:134/175). The card only happens to look populated because the screen passes the locally-scanned string separately in some paths, but the model's content is dead.
fix: In QrScanResult.fromJson read json['raw_content'] (with json['content'] as a secondary fallback). Better: have the backend also include `content` as an alias, but the client fix is sufficient and matches the wire format.

title: Risk score never binds (risk_score vs threat_score) — risk bar permanently 0%
detail: Backend emits the computed score (0-100) as `threat_score`. QrScanResult.fromJson reads json['risk_score'] (line 427) -> always 0.0. QrResultCard binds the progress indicator value and the '%' label to result.riskScore (qr_widgets.dart:121,131), so the risk meter always shows 0% even for a critical QR. Note the backend score is 0-100 while the UI multiplies by 100 (line 131) expecting 0-1, a second scaling mismatch.
fix: Read json['threat_score'] in fromJson and normalize: riskScore = (threat_score)/100.0 so the 0-1 progress bar and *100 label render correctly. Verify _getRiskColor thresholds accordingly.

title: Recommendation never binds (recommendation singular vs recommendations array)
detail: Backend returns `recommendations []string` (and `warnings []string`). Flutter model has a single `recommendation` String read from json['recommendation'] (line 434), which never exists -> always null. QrResultCard gates an entire recommendation panel on result.recommendation != null (qr_widgets.dart:81), so the backend's contextual safety guidance (e.g. 'Do not proceed with this QR code', 'Triple-check the wallet address') is never shown. The backend `warnings` array is also completely dropped by the client.
fix: Add List<String> recommendations and List<String> warnings to QrScanResult, parse json['recommendations'] and json['warnings'] as lists, and render them in QrResultCard. Keep a derived `recommendation` getter (recommendations.firstOrNull) if the single-field UI must stay.

title: analyzed_at never binds (analyzed_at vs scanned_at) — timestamp always 'now'
detail: Backend emits `scanned_at`; fromJson reads json['analyzed_at'] (line 436) and falls back to DateTime.now(). The server-side analysis timestamp is silently discarded.
fix: Read json['scanned_at'] (fallback json['analyzed_at'] then now()).

title: QR scan history persistence is stubbed — history lost on app restart
detail: loadHistory() and _saveHistory() are empty bodies with 'TODO: Load/Save from persistent storage'. init() calls loadHistory() (line 96) and clearHistory/removeFromHistory call _saveHistory(), but nothing is read or written. The History tab and Stats tab are entirely in-memory; every scan record and all stats (totalScanned, threatsFlagged, phishingBlocked, etc.) are wiped on every cold start. The Stats tab and 'Recent Threats' are therefore non-durable.
fix: Implement persistence using the app's storage layer (shared_preferences / Hive / secure storage as used elsewhere). Serialize QrScanEntry (id, content, contentType, scannedAt, result.toJson, isPending=false) to a JSON list on _saveHistory and rehydrate on loadHistory. Add a toJson to QrScanResult/QrThreat for round-tripping. Call notifyListeners after load.

title: 'Report False Positive' / 'Report submitted' buttons are dead no-op UI
detail: The 'Report False Positive' button's onPressed just pops the sheet and shows a 'Report submitted' snackbar — it never calls any provider or API. There is no report method on QrProvider, no scanQrReport on OrbGuardApiClient, and no backend route for reporting false positives (router.go /qr block has scan, scan/batch, check-url, preview, content-types, threat-types, suspicious-tlds, url-shorteners, stats — no report). The user is told their report was submitted when nothing happened.
fix: Either (a) add a real reporting flow: backend POST /api/v1/qr/report-false-positive handler+service that records the indicator/content for review, a QrProvider.reportFalsePositive method, and an OrbGuardApiClient method; or (b) if reporting is out of scope, remove the button and the misleading 'Report submitted' snackbar so the UI doesn't claim a non-existent action.

title: Backend /qr/preview and /qr/check-url routes have no Flutter client method
location: APP lib/services/api/api_config.dart:113-118 / orbguard_api_client.dart (no method) vs LAB internal/api/router.go:494,497
detail: api_config.dart defines qrScanBatch and qrPreview endpoints, and the backend exposes POST /qr/preview, POST /qr/check-url, plus GET content-types/threat-types/suspicious-tlds/url-shorteners/stats. The only client method implemented is scanQrCode (orbguard_api_client.dart:668). qrScanBatch/qrPreview constants are unused and check-url/preview/stats are never invoked. Not a correctness bug for the primary scan path, but these backend capabilities are dead from the client side (e.g. a lightweight 'preview before opening' is wired server-side but unreachable).
fix: If these features are intended, add corresponding client methods (previewQr, checkQrUrl, getQrStats) and surface them (e.g. a non-destructive preview before opening a URL). If not intended, remove the unused qrScanBatch/qrPreview endpoint constants to avoid implying coverage that doesn't exist.

title: Anomalies tab endpoint is a wrong-contract alias returning stats, never anomalies
detail: App getAnomalies() (APP lib/services/api/orbguard_api_client.dart:1506-1514) does GET /ml/anomalies and reads response.data['anomalies']. The route api.Get("/ml/anomalies", r.handlers.ML.GetStats) maps to GetStats, which returns an MLServiceStats object (models_loaded, models[], total_predictions...) with NO 'anomalies' key. The cast yields null -> [] every time, so the Anomalies tab in ml_analysis_screen.dart always shows the 'No Anomalies' empty state. There is a real DetectAnomalies handler at ml.go:53 but it (a) is at POST /ml/anomalies/detect, not GET /ml/anomalies, and (b) is itself a stub (see separate finding).
fix: Add a GET /ml/anomalies handler that fetches recent indicators from repos.Indicators.List, calls MLService.DetectAnomalies, and returns {"anomalies":[...]} with id/title/description/model/severity/anomaly_score/detected_at fields matching AnomalyDetection.fromJson. Remove the GetStats alias at router.go:721.

title: Insights tab endpoint is a wrong-contract alias returning stats, never insights
detail: App getMLInsights() (APP orbguard_api_client.dart:1517-1525) does GET /ml/insights and reads response.data['insights']. Route api.Get("/ml/insights", r.handlers.ML.GetStats) returns MLServiceStats with no 'insights' key, so the cast yields [] always. The Insights tab (_buildInsightsTab) permanently shows 'No insights available yet'. No backend code anywhere generates MLInsight objects (icon/title/insight/color).
fix: Implement a real GetMLInsights handler/service method that derives human-readable insights from GetStats + recent anomaly/cluster results (e.g. 'Anomaly rate 4.2% over last 1k indicators', top cluster, model accuracy) and returns {"insights":[{id,icon,title,insight,color}]}. Replace the GetStats alias at router.go:720.

title: ML models are never trained — no auto-train scheduler despite AutoTrain:true
detail: DefaultMLServiceConfig sets AutoTrain:true, TrainInterval:24h, MinTrainingSize:100 (ml_service.go:57-59) but nothing ever reads AutoTrain or starts a training ticker. main.go constructs mlService at line 171 and only spawns goroutines for HTTP/gRPC/aggregator/scheduler (lines 289-333) — none call mlService.Train(). Training only happens if a client POSTs /ml/models/train, which the Flutter app never does. Consequently isolationForest/kmeans/randomForest IsTrained() is always false: AnalyzeValue->EnrichIndicator (ml_service.go:83) returns a result with nil AnomalyScore, nil ClusterAssignment, nil SeverityPrediction, and GetStats reports models_loaded=0 with every model Status='not_trained'. The Models tab shows models with 0% accuracy/precision/recall.
fix: Honor AutoTrain: in main.go after constructing mlService, start a goroutine that calls mlService.Train(ctx) on startup (once enough indicators exist) and re-trains every config.TrainInterval via a time.Ticker, with context cancellation. Optionally persist/load trained model state so restarts don't lose training.

title: runMLAnalysis posts empty body to AnalyzeValue which requires value+type and 400s
detail: The screen's 'Run Analysis' button (_runAnalysis, ml_analysis_screen.dart:429) calls runMLAnalysis() which POSTs /ml/analyze with NO body. The backend AnalyzeValue handler (ml.go:209-239) decodes {value,type} and returns 400 'value is required' when value is empty. Even on success AnalyzeValue returns a single MLEnrichmentResult (indicator_id, features, anomaly_score...) — it has no 'anomalies' array, yet _runAnalysis reads result['anomalies'] (screen line 440). So Run Analysis either throws (snackbar 'Analysis failed') or silently adds nothing. The button is effectively dead.
fix: Either (a) point runMLAnalysis at a new POST /ml/analyze/run endpoint that runs batch DetectAnomalies over recent indicators and returns {"anomalies":[...]}, or (b) make the UI collect a value+type and call AnalyzeValue then map the single MLEnrichmentResult into the anomaly card model. Align the response key with what _runAnalysis consumes.

title: DetectAnomalies handler is a stub — returns a placeholder message, never runs detection
detail: DetectAnomalies decodes indicator_ids then, instead of loading those indicators and calling s.service.DetectAnomalies, responds with {"message":"anomaly detection requires indicators to be fetched from database","count":N}. The real MLService.DetectAnomalies (ml_service.go:146) exists and works but is never invoked. Same pattern in ClusterIndicators (ml.go:71-92, returns 'clustering requires indicators to be fetched...') and PredictSeverity (ml.go:95-109, returns 'severity prediction requires indicators...').
fix: In DetectAnomalies/ClusterIndicators/PredictSeverity, fetch the indicators for req.IndicatorIDs via repos.Indicators (add a GetByIDs or loop GetByID), then call the corresponding MLService method and return the real result struct. Remove the placeholder message responses.

title: On-device TFLite ML stack is entirely dead/unwired and falsely reports models loaded
detail: MlService, TFLiteMLService, BehaviorAnalyzer, SmsClassifier, UrlClassifier are not imported or used by any screen/provider (grep across lib/ excluding services/ml/ returns nothing) — the ML Analysis screen uses only the REST API. Moreover this stack is non-functional: there is no 'tflite'/'tflite_flutter' dependency in pubspec.yaml, no assets/models/ directory, no pubspec asset declaration, and no native handler for MethodChannel('com.orbvpn.orbguard/tflite'). TFLiteMLService.loadModel always falls through to PlatformException->fallback. Each classifier's loadModel() is a stub with 'TODO: Load actual TFLite model when available' that sets _isModelLoaded=true while only running heuristics (behavior_analyzer.dart:129-131, sms_classifier.dart:89-93, url_classifier.dart:89-92). So MlService.modelsLoaded and ModelInfo.isLoaded report true despite no model existing — a dummy 'loaded' status presented as real.
fix: Either remove this dead on-device ML code, or wire it for real: add tflite_flutter to pubspec, ship real .tflite models under assets/models with asset declarations, implement the native com.orbvpn.orbguard/tflite channel (Android/iOS) or use the Dart Interpreter directly, and have loadModel set isModelLoaded based on actual asset/interpreter load success (false on failure) rather than hardcoding true. Then surface results in a screen/provider.

title: Models tab reads model fields (accuracy/precision/recall/description/is_enabled) the backend never returns
detail: MLModel.fromJson expects name, type, description, accuracy, precision, recall, is_enabled. The backend GET /ml/models -> GetStats returns MLServiceStats.Models as []MLModelInfo (model name, version, type, trained_at, training_size, accuracy(omitempty), parameters, feature_names, status). It has NO description, precision, recall, or is_enabled fields, and accuracy is omitted when 0 (untrained). So every model card shows 0% accuracy/precision/recall, blank description, and defaults is_enabled=true. The Switch toggle (screen line 181-185) only flips local setState and is never persisted to any endpoint (no enable/disable API call) — a no-op control.
fix: Extend MLModelInfo (or a dedicated /ml/models response DTO) with description/precision/recall/is_enabled, populate precision/recall from RandomForest training metrics, and add a POST /ml/models/{model}/toggle endpoint wired to the Switch so enable/disable persists. Alternatively trim MLModel to fields the backend actually provides.

title: Heuristic anomaly path in EnrichIndicator silently yields empty result when untrained (no fallback)
detail: EnrichIndicator guards each prediction behind IsTrained() with no else branch, so on the (current, permanent) untrained state it returns MLEnrichmentResult with only Features populated and AnomalyScore/ClusterAssignment/SeverityPrediction nil — with HTTP 200. Callers (AnalyzeValue) get a success response that looks valid but contains no analysis, masking the untrained condition. There is no signal to the client that models are not ready.
fix: When no model IsTrained(), either return a 503/'models not ready' status or set a result.Status='untrained' flag the client can surface, and/or fall back to the Scorer (scorer.go ScoreIndicator) for a deterministic heuristic severity so the response is never silently empty.

title: Threat Graph screen loads from non-existent /graph/nodes endpoint
location: APP lib/screens/graph/threat_graph_screen.dart:39 -> lib/services/api/orbguard_api_client.dart:1644 (getGraphNodes -> GET /api/v1/graph/nodes via api_config.dart:652) ; backend router LAB internal/api/router.go:258-284
detail: ThreatGraphScreen._loadGraphData() calls _apiClient.getGraphNodes() which GETs /api/v1/graph/nodes and expects response.data['nodes']. The backend /graph route group (router.go lines 258-284) registers correlation/{id}, related/{id}, temporal/{id}, shared-infrastructure, detect-campaigns, ttp-similarity, traverse(POST), search(POST), relationship(POST), sync(POST), build-relationships(POST), stats(GET) -- but NO /graph/nodes. Every load returns 404, the screen's try/catch sets _error and renders an empty graph (0 entities). The whole screen is dead in production despite the Neo4j data existing.
fix: Add a real handler: GraphHandler.GetNodes -> GraphService method that queries Neo4j for nodes (optionally filtered by ?query / ?type) and returns {"nodes": [...]} with fields id,name,type,relation_count,confidence,first_seen matching GraphNode.fromJson. Register graph.Get("/nodes", r.handlers.Graph.GetNodes). Alternatively repoint the client to an existing route (e.g. /graph/traverse or /graph/stats) and adapt the screen's parsing -- but a dedicated /nodes endpoint is the correct fix since the screen needs a node list.

title: Threat Graph screen loads relations from non-existent /graph/relations endpoint
location: APP lib/screens/graph/threat_graph_screen.dart:40 -> lib/services/api/orbguard_api_client.dart:1658 (getGraphRelations -> GET /api/v1/graph/relations via api_config.dart:656) ; backend router LAB internal/api/router.go:258-284
detail: getGraphRelations() GETs /api/v1/graph/relations and expects response.data['relations']. No /graph/relations route is registered in router.go. The call 404s; _relations stays empty so the entity-details bottom sheet (_showEntityDetails) never shows any related entities and the visualization counter shows 0 relationships. Combined with the /graph/nodes gap, the entire Threat Graph feature is non-functional.
fix: Add GraphHandler.GetRelations -> GraphService.GetRelations querying Neo4j relationship edges (optionally ?node_id) returning {"relations": [{id,source_id,target_id,relation_type}]} matching GraphRelation.fromJson. Register graph.Get("/relations", r.handlers.Graph.GetRelations).

title: Correlation screen loads results from non-existent GET /correlation endpoint
location: APP lib/screens/correlation/correlation_screen.dart:41 -> lib/services/api/orbguard_api_client.dart:1731 (getCorrelationResults -> GET /api/v1/correlation) ; backend router LAB internal/api/router.go:315-336
detail: CorrelationScreen._loadResults() calls getCorrelationResults() which GETs /api/v1/correlation expecting response.data['results']. The /correlation route group registers POST "/" (Correlate), /batch, /analyze, GET /indicator/{id}*, /campaigns/*, /cluster, /stats -- there is NO GET on the /correlation root. The GET 404s, the screen errors and shows the empty state permanently. Additionally the expected response shape (results[] with title/engine/description/linked_entities/related_indicators) does not match anything the CorrelationEngine produces (it returns correlations[] with type/strength/confidence/indicators), so even a corrected route would need shape mapping.
fix: Either (a) add GET /correlation that returns recent persisted correlation events shaped as {"results":[{id,title,description,engine,confidence,linked_entities,timestamp,related_indicators}]}, requiring the engine to persist correlation events (currently they are computed on-demand and not stored), or (b) redesign the screen to POST to /correlation/ with selected indicator IDs and map CorrelationResponse.Correlations -> the UI model. A persistence layer for correlation events is needed for the 'results list' UX to be real.

title: Correlation screen 'Run Correlation' button posts to non-existent /correlation/run
location: APP lib/screens/correlation/correlation_screen.dart:321 (_runCorrelation) -> lib/services/api/orbguard_api_client.dart:1745 (runCorrelation -> POST /api/v1/correlation/run) ; backend router LAB internal/api/router.go:315-336
detail: The primary action button (toolbar play icon line 72, and the empty-state 'Run Correlation' button line 298) call _runCorrelation -> runCorrelation() which POSTs /api/v1/correlation/run and reads result['correlations']. No /correlation/run route exists. The POST 404s, _isCorrelating resets, and a 'Correlation failed' snackbar is shown every time. The screen's core feature button does nothing functional. Also note runCorrelation() sends no body, but the real Correlate handler (POST /correlation/) requires indicator_ids or indicator_values and 400s otherwise -- so there is no way to invoke correlation from this screen as written.
fix: Wire _runCorrelation to a real flow: let the user pick/scope indicators, then POST /api/v1/correlation/ with {indicator_ids:[...]} (or /analyze with a value), and map the returned CorrelationResponse into CorrelationResult cards. If a one-click 'run over recent indicators' is desired, add a backend POST /correlation/run that selects recent indicators server-side, runs the engine, persists events, and returns them shaped as the UI expects.

title: Graph search dialog is a dead control (never calls /graph/search)
detail: The search dialog's onSubmitted only does setState(() => _selectedEntity = value); it never calls the backend. The real POST /api/v1/graph/search route (router.go:275, handler SearchGraph) is never invoked from the client (no graphSearch method is used anywhere outside api_config). Setting _selectedEntity merely changes _buildEntityList to show all already-loaded nodes instead of the first 10 (line 179) -- it does not filter by the query at all. So 'search' neither queries the backend nor filters locally by the typed text; it is a no-op search.
fix: Implement a real search: add a graphSearch client method calling POST /api/v1/graph/search with {query, max_depth, limit}, parse GraphQueryResult, and populate _nodes/_relations from the result; or at minimum filter the local _nodes list by name/value against _selectedEntity. Note the backend SearchGraph itself (graph.go:407) is a weak proxy that just feeds query as a traversal StartNodeID (TODO at line 423 'use Neo4j full-text search') -- a proper full-text Cypher search should back it.

title: CalculateTTPSimilarity returns hardcoded placeholder zeros
detail: GetTTPSimilarity handler (graph.go:320) -> graphService.CalculateTTPSimilarity returns a fixed struct {SharedTactics:[], SharedTechniques:[], Similarity:0.0} with an explicit comment '// For now, return a placeholder'. The GET /api/v1/graph/ttp-similarity endpoint therefore always reports zero similarity and no shared techniques regardless of the two actors passed -- fake data presented as a real analysis result.
fix: Implement a real Cypher query in GraphRepository (e.g. MATCH (a1:ThreatActor)-[:USES]->(t:Technique)<-[:USES]-(a2:ThreatActor) ...) to collect each actor's MITRE techniques/tactics, compute Jaccard similarity over the technique sets, and return the actual shared lists and score. Requires syncing actor->technique edges into Neo4j (extend syncActors / build-relationships to create USES edges from MITRE tags).

title: Threat hunting provider reuses the same broken graph/correlation endpoints
detail: threat_hunting_provider.dart depends on the same three non-existent endpoints (GET /graph/nodes, GET /graph/relations, GET /correlation) to build its graph view and _correlationRules. All three calls 404 in production, so any threat-hunting UI driven by this provider's graph/correlation state is also empty/broken -- the gap is not isolated to the two screens.
fix: Fixing the missing /graph/nodes, /graph/relations and the GET /correlation contract (findings above) resolves this provider too. Until then, the provider's graph/correlation sections will silently render empty; ensure the provider surfaces the load error rather than swallowing it.

title: Campaigns list always empty: client reads json['items'], backend returns 'data'
detail: listCampaigns() parses the response via PaginatedResponse.fromJson which reads json['items'] (orbguard_api_client.dart:2213). CampaignsHandler.List returns {"data": campaigns, "total": total} (campaigns.go:54). 'items' is therefore always null, so the Campaigns screen (campaigns/campaigns_screen.dart:37) always shows zero campaigns even though the backend returns real DB rows or DefaultCampaigns().
fix: Make the envelope consistent: either change campaigns.go (and actors.go) to return {"items":[...],"total":N,"page":..,"limit":..,"has_more":..}, or change PaginatedResponse.fromJson to accept json['items'] ?? json['data']. Preferred: update backend handlers to emit the documented paginated envelope used elsewhere (intelligence.go uses ListResponse with 'data' — pick ONE convention and align the Dart parser to it).
domain: Threat Hunting / Actors / Campaigns / Intelligence / Sources / MITRE

title: Threat Actors list always empty: same 'items' vs 'data' mismatch
detail: listActors() uses PaginatedResponse.fromJson (reads 'items'); ActorsHandler.List returns {"data": actors, "total": total}. The Threat Actors screen (actors/threat_actors_screen.dart:42) therefore always renders empty even though the backend serves DB actors or DefaultThreatActors().
fix: Align the response envelope as above (return 'items' from actors.go or read json['items'] ?? json['data'] in PaginatedResponse.fromJson). Also add page/limit/has_more to the actors/campaigns responses so totalPages/hasMore compute correctly.
domain: Threat Hunting / Actors / Campaigns / Intelligence / Sources / MITRE

title: Intelligence Sources screen permanently empty: client reads ['sources'], backend returns ['data']
