The backend computes rich ForensicResult objects (with UUIDs and device IDs) but discards them after the HTTP response — no DB table, no GET /forensics/results/{device_id} endpoint, so neither audit trail nor cross-device history exists for a security product where evidence preservation is explicitly recommended in its own remediation text ('preserve evidence').
fix: Persist results server-side (forensic_results table keyed by device_id/result id) in the handlers after analysis, add GET list/detail endpoints, and have the provider hydrate _analysisHistory from the API (plus optional local cache via shared_preferences/sqflite for offline).

title: Fake progress phases: provider simulates 'Extracting artifacts... / Checking IOC database...' with hardcoded 500ms delays
detail: _runAnalysis loops through four hardcoded phase strings with Future.delayed(500ms) each, advancing the progress bar to 80% before the API call even starts (comment: 'Simulate progress phases'). The progress UI in forensics_screen.dart:204-257 presents these as real analysis stages.
fix: Drop the simulated phases; show an indeterminate spinner during the request, or implement real progress (server-sent events / polling a job id for long-running uploads once the upload architecture exists).

title: GetIOCStats response keys don't match what the app renders (no pegasus/predator/stalkerware/other breakdown)
detail: Backend stats are {domains, ips, hashes, path_patterns, process_patterns, total}; the app reads stats['pegasus'], ['predator'], ['stalkerware'], ['other']. Once the URL is fixed, the IOC category cards would all show 0 with only 'total' populated (~34).
fix: Extend GetStats to count IOCs by Campaign ('pegasus', 'predator', 'stalkerware', other) across all types and emit those keys, or change the app to render the type-based keys the backend provides.

title: QuickCheck iOS indicators guarantee false positives ('bh' and '/private/var/db/' substring match)
detail: QuickCheck flags any iOS log containing the substring 'bh' (a common bigram appearing inside countless words/hex strings) or '/private/var/db/' (a standard iOS path present in virtually every legitimate shutdown.log/sysdiagnose) as suspicious with 0.85 confidence. Unlike the proper parsers (which use word-boundary regexes like `/bh$|/bh/` and `^bh$`), this is naive substring search, so quick-check will report nearly every real device as suspicious.
fix: Reuse the IOCScanner's compiled patterns (pathPatterns/processPatterns) for quick check instead of raw substrings; at minimum use word-boundary regexes (`\bbh\b` as a standalone process token, `/private/var/db/[a-f0-9]{32,}` for paths).

title: Upload handlers use a single file.Read with swallowed error — large uploads silently truncated
detail: `n, _ := file.Read(buf)` performs one Read call and ignores the error; multipart file readers may return fewer bytes than available per call, so logs can be silently truncated and analyzed partially, and any read error is discarded. (These upload routes are also currently unused by the Flutter app, which pastes text instead.)
fix: Use data, err := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(file, 10<<20)) with proper error handling, and wire the app's file-based flows to these upload endpoints.

title: loadCapabilities result is fetched but never used by any UI
location: APP lib/providers/forensics_provider.dart:119,160-168 (capabilities fetched/stored, no consumer in forensics_screen.dart)
detail: init() fetches /forensics/capabilities on every screen open and stores it in _capabilities, but nothing reads it — the screen decides which analysis buttons to show purely from Platform.isIOS/isAndroid. Wasted request and a getter with no consumers.
fix: Either gate the analysis buttons on the reported capabilities (e.g., hide sysdiagnose if backend reports it unsupported) or remove the capabilities fetch from init().

title: Compliance control assessments are simulated with pseudo-random scores
detail: assessControl computes score as 75 + len(ControlID)%25 with canned 'Automated assessment passed' evidence ('Simulated assessment logic ... assign random scores for demonstration'). assessDeviceForFramework hardcodes Score 80.0 / 8 passed / 2 failed for every device. AssessDevicePosture hardcodes OS=85, App=78, Network=90, Behavior=82, Compliance=88 for all devices. Compliance reports and Zero Trust access decisions are therefore fabricated data presented as real.
fix: Implement real assessment logic: query device security state (encryption, passcode, root/jailbreak, OS patch level, threat events from repos), map findings to each framework control, and compute scores from actual evidence. Wire AssessDevicePosture to the device_security and app analysis services instead of constants.

title: App enterprise endpoints have no backend routes: overview and compliance screens always fail
detail: App calls GET /api/v1/enterprise/events, GET /enterprise/devices, GET /enterprise/compliance/controls, POST /enterprise/compliance/reports/generate, GET /enterprise/policies, POST /enterprise/policies/{id}/assign-groups|assign-devices|unassign, POST /enterprise/devices/{id}/evaluate-compliance, POST /enterprise/byod/enroll, GET/POST byod status/unenroll, GET/POST /enterprise/devices/{id}/ownership. None are registered in router.go (policies live only at /enterprise/zerotrust/policies; report generation is POST /enterprise/compliance/reports). Because enterprise_overview_screen.dart:41-45 and compliance_reporting_screen.dart:43-47 use Future.wait, one 404/405 makes the whole screen show the error state every time.
fix: Add the missing routes/handlers in router.go + enterprise.go (events feed, device health list, compliance controls list, reports/generate alias, policy CRUD at /enterprise/policies with assignment endpoints, BYOD enroll/status/unenroll, device ownership), or change ApiEndpoints to the existing zerotrust/compliance paths and adjust payloads.

title: Compliance report generation contract mismatch (payload and supported frameworks)
detail: App POSTs {frameworks: [...], format} to /compliance/reports/generate (route doesn't exist); backend GenerateComplianceReport expects {framework, start_date, end_date}. Even if routed, framework would decode empty and fail. Backend GenerateReport only supports GDPR, SOC2, CIS yet GetSupportedFrameworks advertises HIPAA/PCI-DSS/ISO27001/NIST and the app dialog (compliance_reporting_screen.dart:709) offers them — generating those returns 'unsupported framework'. Report generation can never succeed end-to-end.
fix: Align the request contract (single framework + date range, or support multi-framework + format server-side), register the /reports/generate route, and either implement HIPAA/PCI/ISO/NIST control sets in models or remove them from GetSupportedFrameworks and the app dropdown.

title: Entire enterprise policy management is an in-app, in-memory mock
location: APP lib/services/security/enterprise/policy_management_service.dart:476-588 (class comment line 477: 'Uses local in-memory storage (enterprise API not yet implemented)')
detail: createPolicy/updatePolicy/deletePolicy/addRule/getViolations/resolveViolation operate only on Dart lists with ids like 'policy-1'. Nothing is sent to the backend and nothing is saved to disk — every policy the user creates in enterprise_policy_screen.dart disappears on app restart. Violations list is always empty (nothing ever populates _violations). The backend has a real zerotrust policy CRUD (router.go:528-537) that is never called.
fix: Wire the service to the backend zerotrust policy endpoints (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /enterprise/zerotrust/policies) with model mapping, persist drafts locally (shared_preferences/sqflite) as a cache, and add a backend violations feed; remove the local id counters.

title: evaluateCompliance silently reports devices COMPLIANT when the API call fails
detail: evaluateCompliance calls /enterprise/devices/{id}/evaluate-compliance which has no backend route, so it always throws; the catch returns {'is_compliant': true, 'violations': []} — a security product asserting compliance as a fallback for an error. Similar swallow-and-default patterns: getBYODEnrollmentStatus returns is_enrolled:false on error, assignPolicyToGroups/Devices return false with the error discarded.
fix: Never default to compliant: surface the error to the UI (rethrow or return an explicit 'unknown/error' status), and implement the backend evaluate-compliance endpoint backed by real device state.

title: SIEM screen 'Connect' creates a fake local connection; remove/toggle/test are no-ops
location: APP lib/screens/enterprise/siem_integration_screen.dart:595-611 (Connect), 719-721 (Remove), 348 (forwarder toggle), 706 (Test Connection onPressed: () {}), 631-648 (Add Forwarder dialog), 741-744 (_showAlertDetails)
detail: Pressing Connect just appends a SiemConnection(isConnected: true) to the local list — no call to POST /enterprise/siem/integrations, no connectivity test; the entered HEC token is discarded. Remove deletes locally only (backend DELETE exists). The forwarder enable switch only does setState. 'Test Connection' is an empty onPressed. 'Add Event Forwarder' dialog's Create button just pops. _showAlertDetails calls Navigator.pop(context) — tapping an alert pops the current route instead of showing details.
fix: Call POST /api/v1/enterprise/siem/integrations on Connect (and reload), DELETE /integrations/{id} on Remove, add a real /test endpoint (or reuse SendSIEMEvent) for Test Connection, implement forwarder CRUD against the backend, and build a real alert-details sheet.

title: SIEM list aliases return wrong JSON shapes — all three SIEM tabs always empty
location: LAB internal/api/router.go:710-712 vs APP lib/services/api/orbguard_api_client.dart:1378-1408 and handlers/enterprise.go:324-331,408-411
detail: GET /siem/connections maps to ListSIEMIntegrations which returns {"integrations": [...]} but the app reads data['connections'] -> always []. GET /siem/forwarders is aliased to the same integrations handler and the app reads data['forwarders'] -> always []. GET /siem/alerts is aliased to GetSIEMStats ({total_integrations, total_events_sent}) and the app reads data['alerts'] -> always []. Even real configured integrations would never display.
fix: Either return keys the app expects (connections/forwarders/alerts with proper objects: name, type, endpoint, is_connected, events_per_minute...) via dedicated handlers, or change the Dart client to read 'integrations' and add a real alerts source; remove the misleading forwarders/alerts aliases.

title: STIX/TAXII feature dead in production: TAXII server disabled, wrong response keys, trailing-slash 404
location: LAB config.yaml:221-222 (taxii_server.enabled: false) + router.go:749-757; APP api_config.dart:316-324; orbguard_api_client.dart:1694-1702
detail: (1) /taxii2 routes are only registered when stix.taxii_server.enabled, which is false in config.yaml — every call from stix_taxii_screen.dart 404s and the whole screen shows the error state. (2) getTaxiiServers parses response.data['servers'] from TAXII discovery, which returns {title, description, api_roots} per spec — Servers tab would always be empty. (3) App requests '/taxii2/collections/' (trailing slash) but chi registers '/taxii2/collections' with no StripSlashes middleware — 404 even when enabled.
fix: Enable taxii_server in config (or register routes unconditionally), add middleware.StripSlashes for /taxii2, and rewrite getTaxiiServers to build the server entry from the discovery document (title/api_roots) instead of a nonexistent 'servers' array.

title: STIX screen Add Server/Sync/Remove/filters are local fakes and dead controls
location: APP lib/screens/enterprise/stix_taxii_screen.dart:583-598 (Connect), 684 (Sync Now onPressed: () {}), 697-699 (Remove local-only), 330-343 (filter chips onSelected: (v) {}), 719-721 (_showCollectionDetails empty body)
detail: 'Connect' appends a TaxiiServer(isConnected: true) to a local list without any discovery request or credential validation (username/password fields are discarded). 'Sync Now' does nothing. Filter chips (All/Subscribed/Published/Read Only) do nothing. Tapping a collection does nothing. There is no persistence of added servers either.
fix: On Connect, perform a real TAXII discovery GET against the entered URL with basic auth, persist the server config, populate collections from it; implement Sync Now to refetch objects; implement filter state; build the collection-details sheet (objects, manifest, can_read/can_write).

title: Webhooks list/enable contracts mismatch backend — list always errors and 'enabled' flag never round-trips
location: APP lib/services/api/orbguard_api_client.dart:1415-1423 + screens/webhooks/webhooks_screen.dart:268-276,501-511 vs LAB handlers/webhooks.go:30-41 + models (webhook json tag 'enabled')
detail: Backend List encodes a bare JSON array of webhooks; the app does response.data['webhooks'] on a List which throws, so the screen always shows 'Failed to Load Webhooks'. The app also sends/reads 'is_enabled' while the model field is 'enabled' (json tag), and reads 'type'/'sent_count'/'last_status' which don't exist (model has total_deliveries/last_error) — even after fixing the array issue, status and counters would be fabricated defaults ('Healthy', 0 sent).
fix: Wrap the backend response as {"webhooks": [...]} (or parse the bare list in Dart), and align field names: enabled, total_deliveries, success/failed_deliveries, last_error; derive 'type' from metadata or drop it.

title: Webhook enable toggle and Test button don't call the existing backend endpoints
location: APP lib/screens/webhooks/webhooks_screen.dart:150-154 (Switch local setState), 413-418 ('Test' button: Navigator.pop + comment '// Test webhook')
detail: Backend exposes POST /webhooks/{id}/enable, /disable, /test and /rotate-secret (router.go:656-659), but the app's enable switch only flips a local boolean and the Test button is a no-op comment. Users believe they enabled/tested a webhook when nothing happened.
fix: Add enableWebhook/disableWebhook/testWebhook methods to OrbGuardApiClient and call them from the switch and Test button, showing the delivery test result.

title: Integrations 'Connect' sends a field the backend doesn't have and wipes the integration config
location: APP lib/screens/integrations/integrations_screen.dart:343-368 + orbguard_api_client.dart:1467-1485 vs LAB services/integration_service.go:131-163 and models (no is_connected field)
detail: (1) List: backend returns a bare array; app reads data['integrations'] -> always throws -> permanent error state. (2) 'Connect' PATCHes {'is_connected': true}; models.Integration has Status/Config but no is_connected, so the value is dropped; worse, UpdateIntegration unconditionally does integration.Config = req.Config, overwriting a configured Slack/Teams/PagerDuty config with the zero value, then validateConfig fails — yet the app shows 'connected successfully' based only on a 2xx/err check. (3) The app has no create/config flow at all (no webhook URL / routing key input), and backend uses enable/disable endpoints the app never calls.
fix: Parse the bare array (or wrap server-side); replace the is_connected PATCH with POST /integrations/{id}/enable|disable; make UpdateIntegration only overwrite Config when non-zero; add a configuration sheet (Slack webhook URL, Teams URL, PagerDuty routing key) using POST /integrations.

title: All enterprise backend state is in-memory only — lost on every restart
location: LAB internal/domain/services/enterprise.go:30-33 ('In-memory config store (in production, use database)'), 317-319, 708-710, 1117-1119; webhook_service.go:25-35; integration_service.go:18-26
detail: MDM integration configs, zero-trust policies, SIEM integrations + event queues, compliance reports and findings, all registered webhooks, and all integrations live in Go maps guarded by mutexes. repos is injected but never used for any of them. A pod restart silently deletes every customer-configured webhook, SIEM connection, policy and compliance report. Queued SIEM events are also lost.
fix: Add Postgres tables + repository methods (webhooks, integrations, siem_configs, mdm_configs, conditional_access_policies, compliance_reports/findings) and load them at service start; the Webhook model already carries db: tags, so wire it through repos. Persist or at least drain SIEM queues on shutdown.

title: MDM device sync and MDM threat alerts are simulated no-ops that report success
detail: SyncDevices marks LastSyncStatus='success' and SendThreatAlert marks alerts as 'sent' while no Graph/Workspace ONE/Jamf API call is ever made (httpClient is constructed but unused for MDM). The POST /enterprise/mdm/integrations/{id}/sync handler responds 200 'Device sync initiated' for a fake sync.
fix: Implement the real provider calls (MS Graph managedDevices with client-credential OAuth, Workspace ONE /api/mdm/devices/search, Jamf /JSSResource/mobiledevices), store synced devices via the repos, and propagate alert delivery failures instead of returning nil.

title: Enterprise stats contract mismatch + hardcoded fake trend percentages on overview
location: APP lib/screens/enterprise/enterprise_overview_screen.dart:698-719 (expects security_score, total_devices, ...) vs LAB models/enterprise.go:615-635 (emits mdm_integrations, siem_integrations, ...); hardcoded trends at enterprise_overview_screen.dart:292-296 ('+12%', '+5%')
detail: Even when /enterprise/stats responds, none of the JSON keys the app parses exist in the backend payload, so the security score, device counts, threat breakdown and per-framework compliance bars would all render 0. The metric cards additionally display literal '+12%' and '+5%' trend strings that are invented in the widget. 'View All' (line 165-168) and the export dialog options (572-600) are no-ops, and health cards have onTap: () {} (393).
fix: Define one EnterpriseOverview DTO on the backend with the fields the dashboard needs (security_score, total_devices, threat severity counts, per-framework compliance) computed from real data; remove the hardcoded trend strings or compute real deltas; implement View All (navigate to events list) and real PDF/CSV/JSON export.

title: Compliance screen dead controls and generate dialog ignores user selections
location: APP lib/screens/enterprise/compliance_reporting_screen.dart:378-392 (download/share onPressed: () {}), 651-661 ('Run Assessment' onPressed: () {}), 731-739 (Generate dialog discards selectedFramework/selectedFormat and calls _generateFullReport()), 746-752 (_showReportDetails/_showControlDetails empty), 754-782 (schedule dialog options just pop)
detail: Report download and share buttons do nothing. 'Run Assessment' does nothing. The Generate Report dialog lets the user pick a framework and format, then calls _generateFullReport() which ignores both. Tapping a report or control shows nothing. 'Schedule Reports' Daily/Weekly/Monthly options just close the dialog — no scheduling exists anywhere.
fix: Pass the dialog's selected framework/format into generateComplianceReport; implement report download/share via a backend report-file endpoint; wire Run Assessment to a real assessment trigger; build report/control detail sheets; implement scheduling server-side (cron) or remove the dialog.

title: Executive protection profiles unwired to backend and lost on app restart; backend detector never configurable
location: APP lib/services/security/executive_protection_service.dart:183-186 (in-memory _executives/_corporateDomains, no storage); LAB internal/domain/services/sms_analyzer.go:38-46 (SetExecutives/SetCompanyDomains never called from any handler; no /executive route in router.go)
detail: Executives and corporate domains added in executive_protection_screen.dart live only in a Dart Map — closing the app erases the BEC protection configuration with no warning. The backend has a parallel ExecutiveImpersonationDetector wired into SMSAnalyzer, but there is no API endpoint to configure executives/domains, so its name-match logic can never fire in production; the two implementations are completely disconnected.
fix: Persist profiles locally (encrypted storage) and add backend endpoints (e.g. POST /api/v1/enterprise/executives, /company-domains) that call SMSAnalyzer.SetExecutives/SetCompanyDomains and store the org context in the DB, syncing the app's profile list with it.

title: 933-line Dart SIEMIntegrationService is dead code targeting a nonexistent endpoint
location: APP lib/services/security/enterprise/siem_integration_service.dart:419-470 (class SIEMIntegrationService; loadConfigurations GETs '/enterprise/siem/configurations'; errors swallowed with '// Use empty list on error')
detail: No screen or provider imports this service (the SIEM screen talks to OrbGuardApiClient directly), and its paths (/enterprise/siem/configurations) don't exist on the backend (routes are /enterprise/siem/integrations). All its CEF/LEEF formatting, batching and delivery-tracking logic is unreachable, and every API failure is silently swallowed.
fix: Either delete it, or make it the single SIEM client used by siem_integration_screen.dart and point it at the real /enterprise/siem/integrations endpoints with surfaced errors.

title: STIX UUID generator is a placeholder returning all-zero UUIDs
location: LAB internal/domain/models/stix.go:419-424 (generateUUIDv4 returns "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"; comment 'For now, use a placeholder that will be overwritten')
detail: NewSTIXBundle (stix.go:115) uses GenerateSTIXUUID, so every STIX bundle exported via CreateBundle gets the identical invalid id 'bundle--00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000', breaking STIX 2.1 object-identity semantics for any consumer that deduplicates by id.
fix: Replace generateUUIDv4 with github.com/google/uuid (uuid.New().String()) — the package is already a module dependency.

title: TAXII endpoints (including object write) are registered without authentication
location: LAB internal/api/router.go:749-757 (taxii2 routes outside the APIKeyAuth group), handlers/taxii.go:92-122 (AddObjects)
detail: When enabled, /taxii2/* is mounted outside the authenticated /api/v1 group with no auth middleware, so anonymous clients can POST STIX objects to writable collections; AddObjects stores them as indicators tagged 'community','taxii-submitted' directly into the indicators repository — an unauthenticated threat-intel injection path. Also the 10MB ContentLength check runs after the body is already decoded, making it ineffective.
fix: Apply APIKeyAuth (or TAXII basic auth per spec) to the /taxii2 router, restrict writes to authorized accounts, and enforce the size limit with http.MaxBytesReader before decoding.

title: Compliance frameworks/reports list fields don't match app models — frameworks always show Disabled 0%
location: LAB internal/domain/services/enterprise.go:1390-1400 (GetSupportedFrameworks returns only id/name/description) and models ComplianceReport (name/generated_at) vs APP compliance_reporting_screen.dart:861-916 (expects is_enabled, compliance_score, total_controls, passed/failed, last/next_assessment; report 'title','format','size')
detail: Even with routes fixed, the Frameworks tab parses is_enabled/compliance_score/total_controls which the backend never sends, so every framework renders as grey 'Disabled' with 0% and the overall score divides by an empty enabled set; report cards would show empty titles and a fabricated default 'PDF'/'0 KB'.
fix: Extend GetSupportedFrameworks to include enablement and live per-framework scores (from stored reports/assessments) and align report JSON (title/format/size or change the Dart model to name/generated_at).

title: VPN connect button is a fake 2-second timer, no VPN ever established
detail: _toggleConnection() runs Future.delayed(2s) then flips _isConnected. No API call (api_client.connectVpn exists but is never used here), no platform VPN/tunnel integration. The screen then shows 'Connected / Your traffic is protected' plus stats, which is entirely false.
fix: Wire the button to a real connection flow: call OrbGuardApiClient.connectVpn()/disconnectVpn() (after adding the missing backend routes) and/or integrate a platform VPN service (NEVPNManager / VpnService) for on-device DNS filtering; reflect real connection state and surface errors.
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: Social media monitoring is fully simulated (fake privacy scores, fabricated impersonation alerts and exposures)
detail: _auditPrivacy returns hardcoded per-platform settings/scores without ever inspecting the real account. _checkImpersonation has the literal comment 'Local simulation for demo' and fabricates an alert ('{username}_official', similarity 0.85) for any username longer than 5 chars. _checkDataExposure invents exposures per platform. Users are shown fabricated security alerts as real findings.
fix: Implement real backend-driven checks: add LAB endpoints (e.g. /api/v1/social/impersonation-check backed by username-similarity search and the existing executive_impersonation.go service), call them from the service, and remove the local fabrication. Until real platform-API privacy auditing exists, present the privacy section as a static checklist, not a computed 'score'.
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: All 18 playbook action handlers are stubs that do nothing but return success
detail: handleSendWebhook/Slack/Teams/Email/PagerDuty/SMS, handleBlockIP/Domain/Hash/URL, handleAddTag/RemoveTag/UpdateSeverity/CreateAlert/EnrichIndicator, handleCallAPI/TriggerPlaybook each just log and return {"x": true}. Comment admits 'simplified implementations'. Playbook executions report success while performing zero real actions — automation is entirely fake despite a real trigger/condition/worker engine around it.
fix: Implement each handler against existing services: route send_webhook through WebhookService.Deliver, send_slack/teams/pagerduty through IntegrationService, block_domain/ip through OrbNetService.AddBlockRule/EmergencyBlock, create_alert through the alerts service, add_tag/update_severity through the indicators repository, call_api via http.Client with config validation, trigger_playbook via TriggerManually.
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: YARA 'Scan Device/Directory/File/Memory' fakes results with hardcoded Mimikatz/CobaltStrike detections
detail: _startScan() never calls the backend (POST /yara/scan, /yara/quick-scan, /yara/scan/apk exist and are real). It waits 2 seconds then inserts a fabricated YaraScanResult claiming matches ['Mimikatz_Generic','CobaltStrike_Beacon'] severity 'high' for every device scan. Users see fake malware detections.
fix: Implement real scanning: enumerate device files/apps (with platform channels), send content/hashes to POST /api/v1/yara/scan or /yara/scan/apk via the api client, and render the actual YARAScanResult (matches, severity, risk_score). Remove the Future.delayed fabrication.
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: Playbooks API contract mismatch: backend returns bare arrays and different field names than the app parses
detail: Handlers List/GetExecutions encode the slice directly (JSON array), but the app reads response.data['playbooks'] / response.data['executions'] — indexing a List with a String throws, so the Playbooks screen always errors or shows empty even with data. Additionally Playbook.fromJson (playbooks_screen.dart:544-557) expects 'trigger' (string), 'steps', 'execution_count', 'is_enabled' while the backend model serializes 'triggers' (array), 'actions', 'total_executions', 'enabled' (models/playbook.go:11-40); PlaybookExecution.fromJson expects 'playbook_name'/'triggered_by'/'steps_completed'/'duration' which the execution model does not provide.
fix: Wrap backend responses as {"playbooks": [...]} and {"executions": [...]} (or adapt the client), and align field names: either add a flattened DTO in the handler (trigger summary string, step list from actions, execution_count, is_enabled) or update the Dart fromJson mappers to the real model keys.
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: /vpn/blocked and /vpn/stats alias responses don't match what the VPN screen parses
detail: GET /vpn/blocked -> ListBlockRules returns {"rules":[...]} but the app reads data['domains'] (and expects 'domain','category','blocked_at' shape) so 'Recently Blocked' is always empty. GET /vpn/stats -> GetDashboardStats returns total_servers/blocked_queries_24h/etc. (models/orbnet.go:277-299) but the app reads 'blocked_count','protected_count','uptime','data_usage' — all stats fall back to defaults (0 / '0m' / '0 MB').
fix: Add app-facing handlers for the /vpn/* aliases that emit the shape the app expects ({domains:[{domain,category,blocked_at}]}, {blocked_count,protected_count,uptime,data_usage}), or update getVpnBlockedDomains/getVpnStats and the screen models to consume rules/dashboard-stats fields.
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: App calls /vpn/status, /vpn/connect, /vpn/disconnect, /dns/status — no backend routes exist
detail: Router only aliases GET /vpn/servers, /vpn/blocked, /vpn/stats. getVpnStatus(), connectVpn(), disconnectVpn(), getDnsStatus() all 404, so the Network Security screen's VPN connect/disconnect controls fail at the backend hop.
fix: Implement /api/v1/vpn/status, /vpn/connect, /vpn/disconnect and /dns/status handlers in orbnet.go (tracking OrbNetClient sessions in OrbNetService) and register them in router.go, or remove these client methods/UI and use a purely on-device VPN implementation.
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: VPN server list: no servers ever registered/persisted and fields (flag, latency) don't exist in backend model
detail: OrbNetService.servers is an in-memory map populated only via POST /orbnet/servers; nothing seeds or persists it, so GET /vpn/servers returns an empty list in any fresh deployment and the Servers tab is permanently empty. Even if populated, OrbNetServer (models/orbnet.go:15-41) has no 'flag' or 'latency' fields and 'load' is float64 0-1 while the app parses json['load'] as int (yields 0).
fix: Persist servers in Postgres (orbnet_servers table + repository), seed real server inventory, add flag/latency (or compute latency client-side) to the API response, and fix the load type/scale mismatch.
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: OrbNet block rules and emergency blocks are in-memory only — lost on restart
detail: AddBlockRule/RemoveBlockRule/EmergencyBlock mutate the in-memory blockRules map only. Manually added rules and emergency blocks vanish on process restart (only indicator-derived rules are re-derived). Rule hit counts and expiry (ExpiresAt is set but never enforced by any sweeper) are also volatile.
fix: Add a dns_block_rules repository (insert/delete/update hit counts), load manual+emergency rules at startup alongside indicators, and add a background job to expire rules past ExpiresAt.
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: Social media 'Report' impersonator is a stub; backend social API hardcoded to nonexistent host and response ignored
detail: reportImpersonator() only debugPrints and returns true — the UI shows success but no report is filed anywhere. _apiBaseUrl is hardcoded to 'https://api.orbguard.io/v1/social' (different host than the configured guard.orbai.world backend and no such routes exist in LAB router.go); even on a 200, the decoded response is discarded ('// Process API response'). _apiKey is never provided (initialize() called with no key), so the HTTP path is dead code.
fix: Add real LAB endpoints under /api/v1/social (report-impersonator persisting to DB, impersonation-check), route the service through OrbGuardApiClient/ApiConfig.baseUrl with auth, and process the response into alerts.
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: Settings are persisted but consumed by nothing — every toggle is functionally inert
location: APP lib/providers/settings_provider.dart (only consumer outside settings screens: lib/main.dart:120 registration)
detail: grep shows no code outside the settings screens reads SettingsProvider. Protection toggles (SMS/URL/QR/app/network/dark-web, auto-block), biometric lock, hide-notification-content, quiet hours, scan frequency/auto-scan, VPN auto-connect/kill-switch/preferred server, and WebSocket/timeout settings are saved to SharedPreferences and never read by any service, scanner, notifier, or the VPN screen. Notably 'Biometric Lock' promises app locking but no biometric gate exists anywhere.
fix: Wire each setting to its feature: gate monitor services on protection flags, implement local_auth biometric lock on app resume, have notification code respect quiet hours/sound/vibration, have scan scheduler read scan settings, and have the VPN layer read vpn settings. Remove any toggle that cannot be honored.
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: Custom API server URL / API key setting never applied to the HTTP client
detail: updateApi() saves api_url/api_key to prefs but never calls OrbGuardApiClient.instance.setBaseUrl() (the only mechanism that updates Dio), and nothing reads 'api_url'/'api_key' prefs at startup, so 'Use Custom Server' has zero effect — the client always talks to the compile-time default https://guard.orbai.world.
fix: In updateApi(), call OrbGuardApiClient.instance.setBaseUrl(settings.serverUrl) when useCustomServer is true (and reset otherwise), apply apiKey as an auth header interceptor, and load these prefs in client initialization.
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: Privacy 'Export Data' and 'Delete All Data' only show snackbars — nothing is exported or deleted
detail: Export Data shows 'Data export started...' with no export logic. Delete All Data confirm shows 'All data deleted' while deleting nothing (no prefs clear, no local DB wipe, no backend call). Falsely confirming data deletion is a serious privacy/GDPR problem for an anti-spyware product.
fix: Implement real export (gather prefs + local stores + backend data into a shareable JSON/zip) and real deletion (SharedPreferences.clear, wipe local databases/caches, call backend account-data deletion endpoint), with progress/failure states.
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: Admin report-review endpoints stubbed: list always empty, approve/reject return success without touching DB
detail: ListReports returns hardcoded empty data (TODO: Fetch from database, line 78), GetReport always 404 (TODO line 93), ApproveReport/RejectReport log and return {success:true} with 'TODO: Update in database' (lines 106, 120). Admin moderation workflow is entirely non-functional while claiming success.
fix: Create a reports repository (user_reports table with status), implement ListReports with status filter, GetReport by id, and Approve/Reject updating status + reviewer + timestamp (and, for YARA submissions, promoting approved rules into YARAService).
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: YARA ParseRule always returns valid=true regardless of content
detail: Comment 'TODO: Implement full YARA parsing'. Any garbage submitted to POST /yara/parse gets {valid:true, message:'rule syntax appears valid'} — a validation endpoint that validates nothing.
fix: Reuse the existing internal yara compiler (used by YARAService.AddRule/LoadRules) to actually parse req.RuleContent and return real validation errors with line/position detail.
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: YARA SubmitRule discards user submissions while replying 'submitted for review'
detail: POST /yara/submit assigns an ID/status then only logs ('TODO: Store in database for review'). The submission object is dropped; nothing for admins to review (and admin ListReports is itself stubbed). Client receives a submission_id that references nothing.
fix: Persist YARARuleSubmission to a yara_rule_submissions table via a repository, and surface pending submissions in the admin reports endpoints for approve/reject -> YARAService.AddRule.
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: Playbooks and executions stored in-memory only — all user playbooks lost on backend restart
detail: playbooks map[uuid]*Playbook and executions []PlaybookExecution live only in process memory despite models carrying db: tags. RegisterPlaybook/UpdatePlaybook/Delete and execution history never touch Postgres, and execution history is an unbounded slice.
fix: Add playbooks/playbook_executions repositories, persist on create/update/delete and execution completion, load playbooks at startup, and cap/paginate execution history from DB.
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: Create Playbook dialog is dead — 'Create' button just closes the dialog
detail: The dialog says 'Use the playbook builder to create custom automated responses' but the Create action only does Navigator.pop. No builder UI, no call to POST /playbooks or /playbooks/from-template (both implemented backend routes).
fix: Build a creation flow: fetch GET /playbooks/templates, let the user pick a template + name, POST /playbooks/from-template (add the api_client method), and refresh the list; or implement a full builder posting to POST /playbooks.
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: VPN screen DNS-filter category switches and Settings-dialog switches are no-ops with hardcoded state
detail: Block-category switches have onChanged: (v) {} and hardcoded enabled values (Malware/Phishing/Ads true, Adult/Social false); backend GET /orbnet/categories and rule management exist but are unused. VPN Settings dialog switches are hardcoded value:true with empty onChanged. 'Protection Features' rows are hardcoded enabled=true.
fix: Load categories from GET /api/v1/orbnet/categories, persist per-category enablement (client filter config or backend DNSFilterConfig), wire dialog switches to SettingsProvider.vpn, and derive feature rows from real state.
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: Social media screen dead controls: Dismiss alert, account details tap, and Stop Scan do nothing
detail: Alert 'Dismiss' button has onPressed: () {} (no status change to AlertStatus.falsePositive/resolved). Tapping an account card calls _showAccountDetails which is an empty method body. The header button shows a 'stop' icon and 'Stop Scan' tooltip while scanning but pressing it does nothing (only triggers scan when not scanning).
fix: Implement dismissAlert(alertId) in service/provider mutating alert status; build the account-details modal; add a cancellation flag in SocialMediaMonitorService.scanAllAccounts honored by the stop button.
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: Monitored social accounts are never persisted — list resets every app launch
detail: _accounts is an in-memory map; SocialAccount has toJson/fromJson but no save/load to SharedPreferences, local DB, or backend ever happens. Alerts/exposures are equally volatile.
fix: Persist accounts (and dismissed-alert state) to local storage on add/remove and load in initialize(); ideally sync to a backend monitored-assets endpoint.
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: YARA and Playbook enable/disable switches only mutate local widget state
detail: Both switches call setState on the local model only. Playbook toggle never calls the existing POST /playbooks/{id}/enable|disable routes; YARA toggle has no backend route at all (yara routes have no enable/disable) and resets on refresh.
fix: Playbooks: add enable/disablePlaybook api_client methods hitting the existing routes and revert switch on failure. YARA: add PATCH /yara/rules/{id}/enable|disable updating rule Status in YARAService, then wire the switch.
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: Search bars on Playbooks, Social Media, and YARA screens are decorative (no onSearchChanged)
detail: All three pass hasSearch: true / searchHint to GlassTabPage but omit the onSearchChanged callback (glass_tab_page.dart:127-128 forwards to it), so typing in the search field filters nothing.
fix: Provide onSearchChanged on each screen and filter the rendered lists (playbooks by name/description, accounts/alerts by username, rules by name/category).
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: Desktop Persistence Scanner settings are local widget state — never saved or applied
detail: All ten options (auto-scan on startup, scan interval, launch agents/daemons/login items/kexts/browser extensions/cron, deep scan, hash verification) are plain instance booleans reset on every screen open; nothing persists them and no scanner reads them.
fix: Move these into SettingsProvider (new DesktopScanSettings persisted to prefs) and have the desktop persistence scanning service consult them when building scan targets and scheduling.
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: Terms of Service and Privacy Policy tiles do nothing
detail: Both tiles have onTap: () {} — no URL launch or in-app document. Store review (Apple/Google) typically requires a reachable privacy policy.
fix: Launch the hosted ToS/privacy URLs via url_launcher or render bundled documents in an in-app viewer.
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: OrbNet default block rules include made-up placeholder domains; '24h' query stats are since-boot counters
detail: Default rules seed fictional domains (malware.com, virus-download.net, secure-login-verify.com, tracking.example.com, ads.example.com) alongside the lone real doubleclick.net — placeholder data presented as a threat feed. queriesTotal/queriesBlocked are process-lifetime counters but reported as TotalQueries24h/BlockedQueries24h.
fix: Replace placeholder defaults with a curated real blocklist (or rely solely on indicator-derived rules), and either window the counters over 24h (ring buffer / Redis with TTL) or rename the stats fields honestly.
domain: OrbNet VPN / Social Media / Playbooks / Settings / YARA

title: Login accepts any email/password — no credential verification
detail: POST /api/v1/auth/login validates only that email and password are non-empty, then issues a 24h token and 30d refresh token to ANYONE. There is no user store, no password hash check. It also returns a random hex string as user_id ('user_id': authGenerateToken(16)), i.e. a fake identity per login.
fix: Implement a users table + bcrypt/argon2 password verification in a domain auth service; return the real persistent user_id; reject invalid credentials with 401.
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: APIKeyAuth middleware accepts ANY non-empty bearer token
detail: The validation block is an explicit dev stub: 'if apiKey != secret && apiKey != "dev-api-key" { // For now, allow any non-empty key for development }' — the if-body is empty, so every non-empty Authorization: Bearer X passes for ALL protected /api/v1 routes. All threat-intel, device anti-theft (locate/lock/wipe), enterprise and admin-adjacent data is effectively unauthenticated.
fix: Validate the token against the Redis keys the auth handlers actually create (auth:token:{token} / auth:apikey:{key}) or issue and verify real signed JWTs with config.JWT.Secret; reject unknown tokens with 401. Remove the 'dev-api-key' constant.
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: AdminAuth has hardcoded 'dev-admin-token' backdoor and shares the JWT secret as admin password
detail: X-Admin-Token is accepted if it equals the JWT signing secret or the literal string 'dev-admin-token'. Combined with the permissive APIKeyAuth, anyone can hit /api/v1/admin/* (trigger updates, approve/reject reports) by sending Authorization: Bearer x + X-Admin-Token: dev-admin-token.
fix: Remove the hardcoded literal; implement admin role on real user accounts (claim in a verified JWT or DB role lookup). Never reuse the JWT signing secret as a presentable credential.
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: Device registration token key mismatch — app never authenticates, so every protected endpoint returns 401
detail: The backend RegisterDevice response is {device_id, api_key, expires_at, message}; the app reads data['token'] (null) and only calls saveTokens when token != null. Result: _accessToken stays null, AuthInterceptor.onRequest adds no Authorization header, and APIKeyAuth rejects with 401 'missing authorization header' for ALL /api/v1 protected routes. The registration failure is also silently swallowed (catch at client line 118-120). The whole app↔backend integration is dead on arrival; nothing else in this audit can even be reached in production.
fix: Read data['api_key'] (or change the backend to return 'token') and save it via _authInterceptor.saveTokens; surface registration failure instead of swallowing it; add an integration test asserting an authenticated round-trip to a protected endpoint.
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: Token refresh response key mismatch — refresh always fails and wipes tokens
detail: Backend Refresh returns {'token': ..., 'refresh_token': ...}; the app reads data['access_token'], gets null, returns null, and the catch path calls clearTokens() — so any 401 logs the user out instead of refreshing.
fix: Parse data['token'] (or rename backend field to access_token consistently for login+refresh) and only clearTokens on a definitive 401 from the refresh endpoint itself.
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: Health check path mismatch: app calls /api/v1/health, backend serves /health
location: APP lib/services/api/api_config.dart:284 + orbguard_api_client.dart:1004-1017 vs LAB internal/api/router.go:64
detail: GET /api/v1/health is not registered (only GET /health and /ready). testConnection() catches the 404 and returns false, so the app permanently reports the backend as unreachable even when it is up.
fix: Register pub.Get("/api/v1/health", r.handlers.Health.Check) in router.go (or change ApiEndpoints.health to '/health').
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: Digital footprint / data-broker removal calls /api/footprint/* — missing /v1 prefix, all four endpoints 404
detail: App calls GET /api/footprint/brokers, POST /api/footprint/scan, POST /api/footprint/removal, GET /api/footprint/removal/{id}; backend registers them under /api/v1/footprint/*. Every digital-footprint feature (broker list, scan, removal request, removal status) is fully broken. Also the removal payload differs: app sends {'broker_id': id} while backend RequestRemoval expects the Footprint removal request struct (verify fields when fixing).
fix: Change the four literals to '${ApiConfig.apiVersion}/footprint/...' (add them to ApiEndpoints), and align the POST /footprint/removal request body with handlers/footprint.go RequestRemoval.
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: POST /api/v1/indicators/check alias wired to query-param handler — batch indicator check always 400
detail: The Flutter-compat alias api.Post("/indicators/check", Intelligence.Check) points at a handler that reads r.URL.Query() 'value'/'type' and returns a single CheckResult. The app POSTs {'indicators':[{value,type},...]} and parses response.data['results'] (a list). Every call returns 400 'missing value or type parameter'; even with query params the response shape (single object, no 'results' key) would crash the parser. The core 'check indicators against threat intel' feature is unusable.
fix: Point the alias at Intelligence.CheckBatch (already exists for POST /intelligence/check/batch) and make CheckBatch accept {'indicators':[...]} returning {'results':[...]}, or update the app to call /intelligence/check/batch with the batch payload.
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: All 12 device-security/anti-theft client methods use wrong paths/methods — entire anti-theft feature 404s
detail: Client hardcodes literals that ignore the correct ApiEndpoints functions in api_config.dart:693-733 and the backend contract: GET /device/security/status vs GET /device/{device_id}/security-status; GET+PUT /device/anti-theft/settings vs /device/{device_id}/settings; GET /device/locate vs POST /device/{device_id}/locate; POST /device/command/{command} vs POST /device/{device_id}/command (note: 'lock' accidentally matches /device/{device_id}/lock with device_id='command' — wrong device!); POST /device/status/lost|stolen|recovered vs /device/{device_id}/mark-lost|mark-stolen|mark-recovered; GET /device/location/history and /device/sim/history and POST /device/sim/trusted all omit the {device_id} segment; POST /device/security/audit-os vs POST /device/vulnerabilities/audit. None of locate/lock/wipe/mark-lost/SIM-history works.
fix: Rewrite these 12 methods to take a deviceId parameter and use the existing ApiEndpoints.deviceSecurity/deviceAntiTheft/deviceLocate/deviceCommand/deviceLost/deviceStolen/deviceRecovered/deviceLocationHistory/deviceSimHistory/deviceTrustedSim/deviceAuditOs helpers (deviceAuditOs path is already correct in api_config.dart:733), with correct HTTP verbs (locate is POST).
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: Four forensics client methods use wrong paths — IOC stats, shutdown-log, full analysis, quick check 404
detail: GET /forensics/ioc/stats vs backend /forensics/iocs/stats; POST /forensics/analyze/shutdown vs /forensics/analyze/shutdown-log; POST /forensics/analyze/full vs /forensics/full-analysis; POST /forensics/check vs /forensics/quick-check. The correct paths already exist in api_config.dart:749-777 but the client hardcodes wrong literals. Pegasus/spyware shutdown-log analysis, full analysis and quick check — flagship anti-spyware chains — all 404. (backup/data-usage/sysdiagnose/logcat/capabilities are correct.)
fix: Replace the four literals with ApiEndpoints.forensicsIocStats, forensicsAnalyzeShutdownLog, forensicsFullAnalysis, forensicsQuickCheck.
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: Clipboard check path swapped: app POSTs /privacy/check/clipboard, backend serves /privacy/clipboard/check
detail: checkClipboard() 404s on every call. ApiEndpoints.privacyClipboardCheck (api_config.dart:797) already has the correct path but is unused by the method.
fix: Use ApiEndpoints.privacyClipboardCheck in checkClipboard().
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: Phone reputation queries the literal string 'reputation' instead of the phone number
detail: App calls GET /scam/phone/reputation?number=X; chi matches /scam/phone/{number} with number='reputation' and ignores the query param, so the backend looks up reputation of the string 'reputation' for every user — silently wrong data, not even an error.
fix: Call ApiEndpoints.scamPhoneReputation(phoneNumber) (api_config.dart:821) i.e. GET /api/v1/scam/phone/{number} with the URL-encoded number in the path.
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: SMS false-positive reporting wired to GetStats — user reports are discarded
detail: sms.Post("/report-false-positive", r.handlers.SMS.GetStats) // 'Alias for now, returns stats'. The app's reportSmsFalsePositive (client:1077-1089) sends {message_id, content}; the backend ignores the body and returns stats. False-positive feedback is silently lost.
fix: Implement SMS.ReportFalsePositive in handlers/sms.go that persists {message_id, content, device} to the DB (and feeds the pattern-tuning pipeline), then register it on this route.
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: Rogue-AP trusted-list feature is entirely fake: aliases to unrelated WiFi handlers, DELETE route missing
detail: POST /network/rogue-ap/scan → AuditWiFi (expects a WiFi-audit body, app sends {'access_points':[...]}); GET /network/rogue-ap/trusted → GetWiFiSecurityInfo which returns {'security_types':[...]} (handlers/network_security.go) while the app reads ['trusted_aps'] → always empty list; POST /network/rogue-ap/trusted → AuditWiFi again, so 'add trusted AP' persists NOTHING; DELETE /api/v1/network/rogue-ap/trusted/{id} (client:819) is not registered at all → 404. The whole trusted-access-point management UI operates on fake/no data.
fix: Implement real rogue-AP endpoints in handlers/network_security.go backed by a trusted_access_points table keyed by device: POST /scan comparing nearby APs to known-good, GET/POST/DELETE /trusted with {'trusted_aps':[...]} responses matching the app parser.
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: SIEM connections/forwarders/alerts aliases return wrong-shaped data — app always shows empty lists
location: LAB internal/api/router.go:710-712 + internal/api/handlers/enterprise.go (ListSIEMIntegrations returns 'integrations' key) vs APP orbguard_api_client.dart:1378-1408
detail: GET /siem/connections and /siem/forwarders both alias ListSIEMIntegrations which responds {'integrations':[...], 'count':N}; the app reads ['connections'] and ['forwarders'] → permanently empty. GET /siem/alerts aliases GetSIEMStats (a stats object); the app reads ['alerts'] → permanently empty. Forwarders and alerts are conceptually different resources presented as the integrations list/stats.
fix: Implement distinct handlers (or response envelopes) returning 'connections', 'forwarders' and 'alerts' keys with the real respective data, or change the app parsers to the actual keys and drop the fake forwarders/alerts aliases for real implementations.
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: ML anomalies and insights aliased to GetStats — app parses empty lists from a stats object
detail: GET /ml/anomalies and GET /ml/insights both return MLServiceStats (models_loaded, total_predictions, ... — see internal/domain/services/ml_service.go:421-450), which has no 'anomalies' or 'insights' arrays. The app's getAnomalies()/getMLInsights() read those keys and always get []. The ML insights/anomalies screens render empty forever while appearing 'connected'. (Real anomaly detection exists at POST /ml/anomalies/detect but is unused by these methods.)
fix: Add GET handlers that return persisted anomaly detections and generated insights (e.g. store DetectAnomalies results), or repoint the app to POST /ml/anomalies/detect with real input data.
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: VPN and DNS control endpoints called by app do not exist on backend (6 endpoints)
location: APP lib/services/api/orbguard_api_client.dart:1813-1882 (GET /api/v1/vpn/status, POST /api/v1/vpn/connect, POST /api/v1/vpn/disconnect, GET /api/v1/dns/status, POST /api/v1/dns/enable, POST /api/v1/dns/disable)
detail: None of these six routes is registered in router.go. Any UI toggle for VPN connect/disconnect/status or DNS-protection enable/disable/status backed by these client methods fails with 404 — protection toggles that appear functional do nothing server-side.
fix: Either implement /api/v1/vpn/{status,connect,disconnect} and /api/v1/dns/{status,enable,disable} in LAB (likely proxying OrbNet service state), or remove these client methods and drive VPN/DNS state through the existing orbnet endpoints + on-device VPN control.
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: Supply-chain security endpoints have no backend implementation at all (3 endpoints)
detail: There is no supply-chain handler or route anywhere in orbguard.lab (only a SupplyChainConfig struct in internal/config/config.go:201). Library-vulnerability checking and tracker-signature retrieval are fully unimplemented server-side; all three calls 404.
fix: Create internal/api/handlers/supply_chain.go + a domain service backed by a CVE/OSV dataset and tracker-signature table, register /api/v1/supply-chain/{vulnerabilities,check,trackers} in router.go.
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: Enterprise BYOD, policy-assignment, events/devices/controls and report-generate endpoints missing (13 endpoints)
location: APP lib/services/api/api_config.dart:360-424 + orbguard_api_client.dart:1183-1371 vs LAB internal/api/router.go:510-562
detail: App calls these /api/v1 paths that the backend never registers: GET /enterprise/events, GET /enterprise/devices, GET /enterprise/compliance/controls, POST /enterprise/compliance/reports/generate (backend uses POST /enterprise/compliance/reports), POST /enterprise/policies/{id}/assign-groups, POST /enterprise/policies/{id}/assign-devices, POST /enterprise/policies/{id}/unassign (backend policies live only under /enterprise/zerotrust/policies with no assignment routes), POST /enterprise/devices/{id}/evaluate-compliance (backend: GET /enterprise/compliance/devices/{id}), POST /enterprise/byod/enroll, GET /enterprise/byod/{deviceId}/status, POST /enterprise/byod/{deviceId}/unenroll, GET+POST /enterprise/devices/{id}/ownership. All 404 — BYOD enrollment, policy assignment and device-ownership UIs are dead end-to-end.
fix: Implement and register the BYOD, policy-assignment and ownership routes in handlers/enterprise.go (with persistence), map reports/generate→GenerateComplianceReport and evaluate-compliance→GetDeviceComplianceStatus or new POST handlers, and add events/devices/controls list handlers; alternatively repoint the app to the zerotrust/compliance paths that do exist.
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: Misc app endpoints with no backend route: indicators/{id}, apps/installed, network/threats, graph nodes/relations, desktop GETs, correlation list/run/{id} (10 endpoints)
detail: None of these is registered in router.go: the indicators alias only covers GET /indicators and POST /indicators/check, so indicator detail 404s; /apps/installed, /network/threats, /graph/nodes, /graph/relations, /desktop/persistence|apps|firewall (GET variants) don't exist; /correlation only accepts POST / and GET /indicator/{id} — so getCorrelation('X') (GET /correlation/X), getCorrelationResults (GET /correlation) and runCorrelation (POST /correlation/run) all 404. Threat-graph browsing, indicator detail and correlation screens fail.
fix: Register GET /api/v1/indicators/{id} → Intelligence detail handler; map getCorrelation to GET /correlation/indicator/{id}; add or repoint /graph/nodes and /graph/relations (graph handler has TraverseGraph/SearchGraph that could back them); add GET list endpoints for desktop persistence/apps/firewall or repoint the app to the POST scan endpoints; implement /apps/installed (device app registry) and /network/threats or remove the dead client methods.
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: /vpn/blocked alias returns 'rules' key but app reads 'domains' — blocked-domain list always empty
location: LAB internal/api/router.go:716 + internal/api/handlers/orbnet.go:100-108 vs APP orbguard_api_client.dart:1619-1627
detail: OrbNet.ListBlockRules responds {'rules':[...], 'count':N}; getVpnBlockedDomains() parses response.data['domains'] → always []. The VPN blocked-domains screen silently shows nothing.
fix: Change the app parser to ['rules'] (mapping rule objects to domains), or add a domains projection to the handler response.
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: TAXII collections call uses trailing slash and TAXII routes are conditionally registered
detail: App calls GET /taxii2/collections/ (trailing slash); chi registers /taxii2/collections without a trailing-slash middleware, so the slash variant 404s. Additionally the whole /taxii2 tree is only mounted when config STIX.Enabled && TAXIIServer.Enabled — the app calls it unconditionally, and the discovery parser reads ['servers'] which is not a TAXII 2.1 discovery field (spec returns 'default'/'api_roots').
fix: Drop the trailing slash in ApiEndpoints.taxiiCollections, add middleware.RedirectSlashes (or StripSlashes) to the router, ensure STIX/TAXII enabled in prod config, and parse the real TAXII discovery shape.
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: Silent error swallowing in device registration and token refresh hides the broken auth chain
detail: _registerDevice() catches all exceptions with an empty handler ('Device registration is optional, continue without it') and _refreshAccessToken() swallows all refresh errors. Combined with the api_key/token key mismatch, the app silently runs with zero authentication and no log/telemetry indicating why every API call 401s.
fix: Log and surface registration/refresh failures (at minimum to a diagnostics screen / crash reporting), and make init() report an unauthenticated state to the UI instead of proceeding silently.
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: ~60 backend routes are never called by the app (dead/unused surface)
detail: Routes registered but with no caller in orbguard_api_client.dart/api_config.dart (grep confirmed no other Dart file hits the API): /api/v1/intelligence/* (pegasus, mobile, mobile/sync, community, check/batch, report), /campaigns/{slug}/indicators, /sources/*, /alerts GET list (config const exists, no client method), /url/{stats,whitelist,blacklist,list/{id},block-page,report,dns-rules}, /darkweb/{monitor GET/POST/DELETE,status,breaches,stats,refresh,alerts/{id}/ack}, /apps/{reputation/{package},sideloaded,privacy-report,permissions/dangerous,stats,report}, /network/{wifi/security-types,dns/providers*,dns/configure,arp/check,ssl/check,attacks/types,vpn/config,audit/full,stats}, /graph/{temporal,shared-infrastructure,detect-campaigns,ttp-similarity,traverse,search,relationship,sync,build-relationships,stats,correlation/{id}}, /yara/{scan/apk,scan/ipa,quick-scan(client const exists unused),rules CRUD,parse,submit,categories,stats,reload}, /correlation/{batch,analyze,indicator/{id}/*,campaigns/*,cluster,stats}, /mitre/{tactics/{id},techniques/search,mitigations*,groups*,software*,matrix,navigator/export(POST; app const is GET — also a method mismatch),stats,reload}, /ml/{extract/*,analyze/{id},anomalies/detect,cluster,severity/predict,models/train,features,stats}, /privacy/{camera,microphone,clipboard,screen,trackers*,stats/{device_id},service/stats,patterns,event-types,risk-levels}, /device/{lock,wipe,ring,commands/pending,commands/{id}/ack,location POST,sim POST/GET,selfie,selfies,security-info,vulnerabilities/known,service/stats}, /qr/{scan/batch,check-url,preview(const unused),content-types,threat-types,suspicious-tlds,url-shorteners,stats}, /enterprise/{overview,mdm/*,zerotrust/*,siem/integrations CRUD,siem/events,compliance/findings*}, /orbnet/{rules CRUD,emergency-block,servers CRUD,dashboard,categories}, /forensics/{ios/shutdown-log/upload,android/logcat/upload}, /footprint/{quick-scan,brokers/categories,brokers/{id},removal/batch,stats}, /desktop/{persistence/scan-path,codesign/verify-batch,virustotal/file,virustotal/batch}, /webhooks/{id GET/PUT,enable,disable,test,rotate-secret,stats}, /playbooks/{templates,from-template,{id} CRUD,enable,disable,stats}, /integrations/{id GET/DELETE,enable,disable,test,stats,deliveries}, /scam GET base, /sms/stats (const exists, no client method), /admin/*, /api/v1/streaming/stats, /ready. Not bugs per se, but a large maintained-but-unconsumed surface that suggests unfinished app features.
fix: Decide per group: wire the app screens that these were built for (e.g. intelligence/mobile/sync for offline IOC sync, darkweb monitor CRUD for the monitoring UI, qr/preview, yara/quick-scan), or prune/feature-flag the unused routes; at minimum add contract tests so future app wiring matches these shapes.
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: Login/refresh response field naming inconsistent with app expectations across the whole auth surface
location: LAB internal/api/handlers/auth.go:85-95 + 137-142 vs APP api_config.dart:344-352 / api_interceptors.dart:155
detail: Backend login returns 'token'; the app's refresh parser expects 'access_token'; device registration returns 'api_key'. Three different key names for the same concept guarantee breakage anywhere a token is consumed; there is also no app login UI calling /auth/login at all (grep: no usage of authLogin outside api_config.dart), so the login endpoint is currently dead from the app side.
fix: Standardize on one envelope ({access_token, refresh_token, expires_in, token_type}) across login/refresh/device endpoints and update AuthInterceptor + _registerDevice accordingly; add a login flow in the app if account auth is intended.
domain: API contract cross-check: APP (orbguard Flutter) ↔ LAB (orbguard.lab Go backend)

title: RegisterThreatIntelligenceServiceServer is an empty no-op — entire gRPC service unreachable despite being 'started'
location: LAB internal/grpc/threatintel/types.go:32-35 (called from server.go:47-49, wired in cmd/api/main.go:304-318)
detail: The function body is empty with comments 'This would be implemented by generated code / For now, this is a placeholder'. main.go dutifully creates the listener on :9002, constructs the Server, calls Register(), and Serve()s — but no grpc.ServiceDesc is ever added, so the grpc.Server exposes ZERO ThreatIntelligenceService methods. Every client call returns 'unknown service threatintel.v1.ThreatIntelligenceService'. All 9 real implementations in server.go (which correctly use repos.Indicators, repos.Sources, RedisCache, EventBus) are dead code.
fix: Run `make proto` (Makefile:57-62 already has the protoc --go_out/--go-grpc_out target) to generate threat_intelligence.pb.go + threat_intelligence_grpc.pb.go in proto/threatintel/v1 (package threatintelv1), delete the hand-written types.go, import threatintelv1 in server.go, embed the generated threatintelv1.UnimplementedThreatIntelligenceServiceServer, and call the generated RegisterThreatIntelligenceServiceServer(grpcServer, s). Requires protoc + protoc-gen-go + protoc-gen-go-grpc in CI.
domain: gRPC threat-intel server (LAB internal/grpc/threatintel + proto/threatintel/v1, wiring in cmd/api/main.go, consumers in orbnet/orbmesh/APP)

title: types.go is hand-written placeholder code instead of protoc-generated — message structs cannot be (de)serialized
location: LAB internal/grpc/threatintel/types.go:1-287 (header comment lines 3-5 admits it)
detail: File header: 'This file contains placeholder types that would normally be generated by protoc. In a real implementation, run: protoc ...'. The structs (CheckIndicatorsRequest, ThreatUpdate, Indicator, etc.) are plain Go structs that do not implement proto.Message — no protobuf wire marshaling exists, so even if registration were hand-written, requests/responses could never cross the wire. No *.pb.go file exists anywhere in the repo (verified by find). The .proto go_package targets a DIFFERENT package (orbguard-lab/proto/threatintel/v1;threatintelv1) than the hand-written package threatintel, confirming generation was never run.
fix: Generate code via `make proto`, migrate server.go to the threatintelv1 package types (note: generated code uses getter methods and pointer/optional semantics — e.g. req.GetIndicators(), and field renames like Id->Id stays but flat literals need updating), then delete types.go entirely. Add `make proto` to the build pipeline so drift cannot recur.
domain: gRPC threat-intel server (LAB internal/grpc/threatintel + proto/threatintel/v1, wiring in cmd/api/main.go, consumers in orbnet/orbmesh/APP)

title: ThreatUpdate hand-written type completely diverges from the .proto contract
detail: The .proto ThreatUpdate is {UpdateType update_type; Indicator indicator; Timestamp timestamp} (3 fields, nested Indicator message, UpdateType enum). The hand-written ThreatUpdate is a flat 16-field struct (Id, Type string, IndicatorId, IndicatorValue, Severity, Confidence, CampaignName, SourceSlug...). StreamNewThreats (server.go:239-255) and its heartbeat (server.go:269-272, Type:"heartbeat") build messages that have no representation in the proto schema. Similarly StreamThreatsRequest: proto platforms is repeated Platform (enum) with no tags field; types.go has Platforms []string and Tags []string (types.go:140-147, consumed at server.go:200-205).
fix: After protoc generation, rewrite StreamNewThreats to emit the generated ThreatUpdate{UpdateType: UPDATE_TYPE_NEW, Indicator: modelIndicatorToProto(...), Timestamp: ...}. Either drop the heartbeat (gRPC keepalive params on the server handle this properly: grpc.KeepaliveParams) or add an explicit heartbeat/UpdateType field to the .proto. Decide whether tags filtering belongs in the proto (add field) or remove sub.Tags from the stream handler.
domain: gRPC threat-intel server (LAB internal/grpc/threatintel + proto/threatintel/v1, wiring in cmd/api/main.go, consumers in orbnet/orbmesh/APP)

title: CheckIndicators implemented but unreachable over gRPC
detail: Real implementation exists (batch validation, repos.Indicators.CheckBatch DB lookup, severity/confidence mapping) but is dead code: the no-op registration means clients hit 'unknown service'. The proto comment says this is the OrbNet DNS-filtering entry point — that integration cannot function. Also note the s.repos==nil branch (server.go:64-75) silently returns all-benign results; in production a nil repo should be a startup failure, not a 'nothing is malicious' answer.
fix: Expose via generated stubs (see finding 1). Change the s.repos==nil branch to return codes.Unavailable instead of fabricated 'not malicious' results, since a false-negative on a Pegasus domain is a security failure.
domain: gRPC threat-intel server (LAB internal/grpc/threatintel + proto/threatintel/v1, wiring in cmd/api/main.go, consumers in orbnet/orbmesh/APP)

title: GetThreatStats implemented but unreachable; swallows all repo errors silently
detail: Unreachable for the same registration reason. Additionally both repos.Indicators.GetStats and repos.Sources.GetStats errors are silently ignored (`if err == nil { ... }` with no else, lines 140-163), so a DB outage yields an all-zeros stats response presented as real data. Line 175 also dereferences s.cache.GetJSON without the nil-guard used 8 lines earlier (s.cache != nil at line 167) — nil-pointer panic if cache is nil.
fix: Expose via generated stubs. Log and/or propagate repo errors (return codes.Internal or set a partial-data flag) instead of returning zeroed stats. Guard the second s.cache use at line 175 with the same nil check, or hoist one `if s.cache != nil` block.
domain: gRPC threat-intel server (LAB internal/grpc/threatintel + proto/threatintel/v1, wiring in cmd/api/main.go, consumers in orbnet/orbmesh/APP)

title: StreamNewThreats implemented but unreachable; nil-eventBus fallback silently streams nothing forever
detail: Real EventBus subscription logic exists but no client can open the stream (registration no-op). When s.eventBus is nil it substitutes an empty channel and keeps the stream open sending only heartbeats — a client would believe it is subscribed to live threats while receiving none, indefinitely. Also `defer close(ch)` on a channel the select still reads is fragile ordering.
fix: Expose via generated stubs. Return codes.Unavailable when eventBus is nil rather than a silent dead stream. Replace the manual 30s heartbeat with grpc keepalive once ThreatUpdate matches the proto (heartbeat message doesn't fit the schema).
domain: gRPC threat-intel server (LAB internal/grpc/threatintel + proto/threatintel/v1, wiring in cmd/api/main.go, consumers in orbnet/orbmesh/APP)

title: ShouldBlockDomain implemented but unreachable — the OrbMesh VPN block-check integration cannot work
detail: DB-backed implementation (repos.Indicators.GetByValue) exists but is unreachable. This is the RPC the proto designates for VPN-server DNS blocking (proto line 20-21); orbmesh has zero references to it (verified grep across /Users/nima/Developments/orbmesh), so the entire 'VPN servers block malicious domains via OrbGuard Lab' feature is unbuilt end-to-end. The GetByValue error is also swallowed (err treated identically to not-found), so DB outage means 'allow everything'.
fix: Expose via generated stubs, then build the orbmesh-side gRPC client (dial lab:9002, call ShouldBlockDomain from the DNS/SNI filtering path, with a local cache + fail-open/fail-closed policy decision). Distinguish sql-not-found from infrastructure errors and return codes.Unavailable on the latter.
domain: gRPC threat-intel server (LAB internal/grpc/threatintel + proto/threatintel/v1, wiring in cmd/api/main.go, consumers in orbnet/orbmesh/APP)

title: ReportNetworkThreat implemented but unreachable; failure path returns gRPC OK with success=false
detail: Solid implementation (metadata JSON, Indicator Upsert, EventBus publish) but unreachable. Two quality issues once exposed: (a) on Upsert failure it returns a nil-error response with Success=false (lines 376-381) — callers checking only gRPC status will treat the report as delivered; (b) the no-database branch (lines 406-414) returns Success=true 'Report received (database unavailable)' while the report is dropped on the floor — a fabricated success.
fix: Expose via generated stubs. Return status.Error(codes.Internal/Unavailable) on storage failure instead of OK+success=false, and never return Success=true when the report was not persisted (or buffer to a durable queue).
domain: gRPC threat-intel server (LAB internal/grpc/threatintel + proto/threatintel/v1, wiring in cmd/api/main.go, consumers in orbnet/orbmesh/APP)

title: GetIndicator implemented but unreachable; all repo errors mapped to found=false
detail: Unreachable due to no-op registration. Line 433-437 treats every error from repos.Indicators.GetByValue (including connection failures) as 'indicator not found' at Debug level — a DB outage silently reports known-malicious IOCs as clean.
fix: Expose via generated stubs. Differentiate pgx.ErrNoRows (found=false) from other errors (codes.Internal).
domain: gRPC threat-intel server (LAB internal/grpc/threatintel + proto/threatintel/v1, wiring in cmd/api/main.go, consumers in orbnet/orbmesh/APP)

title: ListIndicators implemented but unreachable; cursor pagination advertised in proto but not implemented
detail: Unreachable due to registration no-op. The proto contract exposes cursor/next_cursor (proto lines 242, 250) but the handler ignores req.Cursor entirely and hardcodes NextCursor: "" with comment 'Could implement cursor-based pagination later' — clients implementing cursor paging per the contract would loop or stall. The Platforms filter from the request (types.go:232) is also silently dropped (not mapped into repository.IndicatorFilter at lines 461-469).
fix: Expose via generated stubs. Either implement cursor pagination (encode last (created_at,id) tuple) or remove cursor fields from the .proto. Map req.Platforms into IndicatorFilter (the repository filter supports platform filtering for the REST path).
domain: gRPC threat-intel server (LAB internal/grpc/threatintel + proto/threatintel/v1, wiring in cmd/api/main.go, consumers in orbnet/orbmesh/APP)

title: GetCampaign serves only hardcoded DefaultCampaigns(), ignoring the real CampaignRepository
detail: Besides being unreachable (registration no-op), the method never touches the database: it iterates models.DefaultCampaigns() (models/campaign.go:162) — a static in-memory seed list — even though a full CampaignRepository with GetByID/GetBySlug exists and is wired into s.repos.Campaigns. Any campaign created/updated via the REST API or aggregator is invisible to this RPC; indicator counts and last_seen are frozen seed values presented as live data. ThreatActor fields in the proto Campaign message are never populated.
fix: Replace the DefaultCampaigns() loop with s.repos.Campaigns.GetByID/GetBySlug (parse uuid for req.Id, slug lookup for req.Slug), fall back to defaults only when repos==nil, and populate threat_actor_id/name via repos.Actors.
domain: gRPC threat-intel server (LAB internal/grpc/threatintel + proto/threatintel/v1, wiring in cmd/api/main.go, consumers in orbnet/orbmesh/APP)

title: ListCampaigns serves only hardcoded DefaultCampaigns(), ignoring repos.Campaigns.List and request pagination
detail: Same pattern as GetCampaign: static seed list instead of CampaignRepository.List(ctx, activeOnly, limit, offset) which already exists with full SQL. req.Limit and req.Offset (proto lines 286-287) are completely ignored, so the proto pagination contract is dead. DB campaigns never appear; seed indicator counts are stale fabrications.
fix: Call s.repos.Campaigns.List(ctx, req.ActiveOnly, limit, offset) with sane limit clamping, convert via a shared modelCampaignToProto helper, and return the repository's total count.
domain: gRPC threat-intel server (LAB internal/grpc/threatintel + proto/threatintel/v1, wiring in cmd/api/main.go, consumers in orbnet/orbmesh/APP)

title: No consumer exists for the gRPC service anywhere in the platform (orbnet, orbmesh, Flutter app)
location: LAB proto/threatintel/v1/threat_intelligence.proto:11-24 (claims 'used by OrbNet for DNS filtering', 'for OrbMesh DNS filtering') vs zero matches in /Users/nima/Developments/orbnet, /Users/nima/Developments/orbmesh, /Users/nima/Developments/orbguard/lib
detail: Grep across orbnet (Go), orbmesh (Go), and the Flutter APP finds no threatintel/ThreatIntelligence client code and no Dart gRPC usage at all. The Flutter app talks to LAB exclusively over REST (lib/services/api/orbguard_api_client.dart). So even after generation+registration, port 9002 would have no callers — the cross-product integration the proto documents (OrbNet/OrbMesh DNS filtering driven by OrbGuard intel) is entirely unbuilt on the client side.
fix: After generating stubs, vendor/import the threatintelv1 package into orbnet and orbmesh, add a gRPC client (with mTLS or API-key interceptor — the current grpc.NewServer() at main.go:304 has NO transport security or auth interceptors either) and wire ShouldBlockDomain/StreamNewThreats into orbmesh's DNS filtering path and ReportNetworkThreat into its traffic analysis. Add server-side TLS creds + auth interceptor before exposing 9002 beyond localhost.
domain: gRPC threat-intel server (LAB internal/grpc/threatintel + proto/threatintel/v1, wiring in cmd/api/main.go, consumers in orbnet/orbmesh/APP)

title: gRPC health service reports threatintel.v1.ThreatIntelligenceService as SERVING when the service is not registered
